prompt.raw:
[{"content":"You are a report card writer for a school teacher. The student notes are the sole source of facts for the report. Every observation, data field, and mark must come from the notes — not from any examples. ## Style & Layout Guide The following are example report cards. Match their tone, voice, vocabulary, section headings, and layout exactly. These examples may differ slightly from the provided additional instructions - in that case, the instructions from the user take precedence. IMPORTANT: Do not copy specific Data field values, Marks, or observations from the examples — only include a Data field or Marks section if that value is present in the student notes. ### Example 1: pdfAvaluacioElize.pdf Term report (3rd term) Data Name: Elise Walter-Tossens Teacher: Theresa Galler Level: Oliver 2024/2025 Absences: 0 Frequency of use (audio): Pass Participation/Motivation Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Good Comment: Elise shows a positive eagerness to engage in her learning, especially during activities like the dice game, where she demonstrated enthusiasm and presence. Despite facing some challenges with vocabulary, she enjoys participating in classroom tasks and activities. Her interests in games and hands-on materials, like flashcards, indicate her motivation to connect with the learning material. These interests can be harnessed to further enhance her learning experience and maintain her enthusiasm. Learning process Assessment (Learning process): Good Comment: Elise is currently facing some challenges with vocabulary acquisition and constructing complete sentences, as she is still working on concepts related to clothing and prepositions. However, she demonstrates significant progress, particularly with prepositions like 'there is' and 'there are'. She performed well on worksheets, indicating a solid understanding of certain exercises. Continuous practice with vocabulary and sentence construction will be essential for her growth moving forward. Behaviour Assessment (Behaviour): Satisfactory Comment: Elise is a friendly and kind student who readily shares materials and assists her classmates, contributing positively to the classroom environment. However, she often needs reminders to focus and tends to chat with peers during lessons, which can disrupt her learning. Despite these distractions, Elise responds well to guidance and refocusing prompts. Overall, her behaviour is manageable, and with continued support, she can further enhance her classroom conduct while maintaining her sociable nature. Marks Oral Test Mark: 7.70 Good. Elise can understand the question and answers either with a single word or with a structure which is only partially correct. --- Final comment Elise has shown a willingness to improve her behaviour. Throughout the year, she has demonstrated kindness and a willingness to help her peers, contributing positively to the classroom. Continued focus on vocabulary and sentence structure will aid her learning. As summer approaches, consistent listening to audio resources will assist her development and readiness for the upcoming year. ### Example 2: pdfAvaluacioMaya.pdf Term report (3rd term) Data Name: Maya Spineux Teacher: Theresa Galler Level: Oliver 2024/2025 Absences: 1 Frequency of use (audio): Pass Participation/Motivation Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Good Comment: Maya comes to class with a cheerful disposition, always smiling and eager to begin her lessons. Her enthusiasm is infectious, and she shows good motivation towards her work, particularly enjoying activities like Pass the Present and Who's Missing. Although she faced challenges with vocabulary and constructing complete sentences, her strong willingness to engage is commendable. Maya participates actively during games and enjoys singing, which reflects her positive approach towards learning. Learning process Assessment (Learning process): Good Comment: Maya has made notable progress in her learning, participating well during exercises such as true or false and memory activities. She made good progress in warm-up activities, showing good grasp of concepts like weather-related tasks. However, she struggled with vocabulary and sentence construction at times. To enhance her learning further, continued practice in repeating vocabulary and engaging in fun exercises will be beneficial in reinforcing her knowledge. Behaviour Assessment (Behaviour): Good Comment: Maya exhibits friendly and cooperative behaviour in class, getting along well with both her classmates and teachers. Her cheerful nature contributes positively to the classroom environment. While she has shown occasional distractions requiring gentle reminders to refocus, she actively engages in group activities and willingly shares materials. Maya has been helpful in supporting her peers, demonstrating kindness and respect in her interactions, which enhances the overall classroom atmosphere. Marks Oral Test Mark: 7.20 Good. Maya can understand the question and answers either with a single word or with a structure which is only partially correct. --- Final comment Maya has had a productive year, demonstrating enthusiasm across a range of activities. While her vocabulary and sentence construction need improvement, her eagerness to participate is commendable. Encouraging her to listen to audio tracks over the summer will surely support her learning progress for next year. ### Example 3: pdfAvaluacioHugo.pdf Term report (3rd term) Data Name: Hugo Theunissen Teacher: Theresa Galler Level: Oliver 2024/2025 Absences: 1 Frequency of use (audio): Insufficient Participation/Motivation Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Good Comment: Hugo demonstrates a good level of motivation during class activities, showing adequate participation and enthusiasm in distinguishing between True and False. He especially enjoyed the dice game and Passing the Present. Although he occasionally struggles to stay awake during story time, he does engage when he is alert. Hugo's willingness to participate, even when needing extra time to repeat answers, is commendable and he contributes positively to the classroom environment. Learning process Assessment (Learning process): Satisfactory Comment: Hugo has shown satisfactory progress in his learning, particularly with true or false exercises and understanding worksheet activities. He performed adequately with vocabulary tasks, although he occasionally appears tired, affecting his engagement during stories. While he successfully repeats instructions when prompted, continued practice with full sentences will enhance his language skills further. Behaviour Assessment (Behaviour): Good Comment: Hugo has a good rapport with peers and respects his teacher, which contributes to a pleasant classroom environment. He displays both kindness and respect for learning materials, vital for maintaining a positive environment. Furthermore, he's capable of taking turns, underscoring his understanding of cooperative conduct in group activities. Marks Oral Test Mark: 6.90 Acceptable. Hugo can understand the question but, in most cases, still does not respond with a complete structure. --- Final comment Hugo has had a good year, demonstrating good participation and developing his skills in various activities. He should continue to listen to audio tracks over the summer to assist in further developing his English skills. This will help him be more engaged and ready for the challenges of the coming year. Overall, Hugo's positive interactions and effort are commendable. ## Student Notes (source of truth — all report content must derive from these) Student: Sophie, Class: Marcia-Wed@14:10 - 2026-04-13: Sophie did very good. response.output:
Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Sophie

Class: Marcia-Wed@14:10

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Excellent

Comment: Sophie continues to bring a positive and confident energy to the classroom. She is consistently eager to participate and shows enthusiasm across all activities, including story retelling, charades and workbook activities. Sophie regularly volunteers answers and stays engaged throughout tasks, contributing to a focused and productive learning environment. She pushes herself to always give her very best.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Excellent

Comment: Sophie has made outstanding progress in her learning and shows a very secure understanding of the topics covered. She consistently answers questions correctly, uses complete sentences, and helps to repeat and retell the story. She remembers what Marcia needs to wear to go to certain places. She was repeating everything in full structures.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Excellent

Comment: Sophie's behaviour in class is excellent. She follows instructions well, works calmly through workbook activities, and remains focused during group games. She is cooperative and supportive, often helping the class by repeating parts of the story and contributing appropriately during discussions. Her positive attitude contributes to a smooth and productive classroom environment.

Final comment

Sophie has had an excellent year. She has shown a positive and confident energy in class, has been consistently eager to participate, and has made outstanding progress in her learning. Her excellent behaviour, cooperation and supportiveness have contributed to a smooth and productive classroom environment.

[{"role":"system","content":"You are grading output according to a user-specified rubric. If the statement in the rubric is true, then the output passes the test. You respond with a JSON object with this structure: {reason: string, pass: boolean, score: number} Examples: Hello world Content contains a greeting {"reason": "the content contains the word 'Hello'", "pass": true, "score": 1.0} Avast ye swabs, repel the invaders! Does not speak like a pirate {"reason": "'avast ye' is a common pirate term", "pass": false, "score": 0.0}"},{"role":"user","content":"

Kids&Us.

Objectives and content of the Sam course, 3rd term

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Thomas
Teacher: [Not provided in notes]
Level: Sam Saturday
Absences: [Not provided in notes]
Frequency of use (audio): [Not provided in notes]

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Good

Comment:

Thomas was attentive and followed instructions when engaged, showing understanding of vocabulary such as moods, genders, and weather. He participated in activities like pairing numbers with spots and repeating phrases from the story "Why are you so sad." However, he was not overly participative and required encouragement to join group routines. He enjoyed the "spots and stripes" activity and story time but showed some uncooperative moments, such as refusing to high-five the teacher after repeated reminders about handling materials.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Good

Comment:

Thomas demonstrated a solid grasp of vocabulary and structures, including moods, genders, and weather. He could count very well and pair numbers with spots. While he needed occasional help to recall full structures or identify animals, he showed improvement in repeating phrases and following instructions. His participation in the "Let's go to the zoo" activity was noted, though he did not perform the gestures like the others.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Good

Comment:

Thomas generally treated classmates and the teacher with respect but showed moments of uncooperativeness, such as refusing to high-five after repeated reminders. He took care of materials when reminded but initially struggled to follow routines without prompting. His behaviour improved when engaged in activities he enjoyed, such as story time and the "spots and stripes" game.

Marks

Oral Test Mark: [Not provided in notes]

Final comment

Thomas showed progress in his learning this term, particularly in vocabulary recall and participation in structured activities. While he required occasional encouragement to fully engage, his enthusiasm for story time and certain games was evident. Continued practice with the audio tracks would help reinforce the vocabulary and structures learned this year.

 Source notes (ground truth — every statement in the report must be traceable to these): [ { "date": "2026-05-14", "summary": "Thomas came in my class today with Jamie, Nicola, Meilin and Élise. Thomas and Meilin were very attentive and did what they were asked, and it was great. Kids&Us

Objectives and content of the Sam course, 3rd term

Term report (3rd term)

Data

  • Name: Thomas
  • Teacher: Clarisse Bydens
  • Level: Sam Saturday 2025/2026

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Good

Comment:
Thomas usually came to class in a good mood and showed interest in the activities. He particularly seemed to enjoy the "Let's go to the zoo" activity, the "Why are you so sad?" story and the routines. However, he was sometimes a bit uncooperative and needed reminders to stay involved and participate with the group. At times, he had to be encouraged to join in with the class, but when focused, he was able to follow along well.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Good

Comment:
Thomas's English knowledge is good. He can understand and repeat the vocabulary and structures worked on in class. He was able to tell the colours, the genders and the moods, and he could also repeat the weather and do the gestures for it. He could count very well and matched the dot cards with the correct numbers successfully. He sometimes needed a little help to remember the full structure, but he showed a good understanding of the content and could respond well when supported.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Acceptable

Comment:
Thomas is a quiet student who can be attentive and cooperative when he is engaged in the lesson. He sometimes needed to be accompanied more closely into the activities and was not always willing to participate with the group. On one occasion, he did not respect the classroom materials and needed several reminders to handle them properly. Nevertheless, he was able to show his understanding during the activities and, when focused, he worked well with the teacher and the class.

Marks

Oral Test Mark: 9.00 Very good. Thomas could do the test very well and showed a good understanding of the vocabulary and structures.

Final comment

Throughout the term, Thomas has shown that he can understand and use the language well, especially when he is focused and willing to take part. He has made good progress in his oral comprehension and in his ability to repeat the structures learnt in class. It would be beneficial to continue listening to the audio track at home in order to keep in mind the vocabulary words and structures learnt throughout the school year. The teacher would like to encourage Thomas to keep improving his participation and consistency in class.

The report must follow the three-section structure (Participation/Motivation, Learning process, Behaviour) plus a Final comment, as shown in the examples. The notes include absences and mixed behaviour — the report should handle these accurately without inventing positive observations. Score 1-5 on: - structure: three sections + final comment, each with Assessment and Comment - grounding: every statement traceable to notes (no invented observations) - absences: absent sessions noted correctly, not fabricated into positive observations - tone: warm but honest, matches examples "}]

Kids&Us.

Objectives and content of the Sam course, 3rd term

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Thomas
Teacher: [Not provided in notes]
Level: Sam Saturday
Absences: [Not provided in notes]
Frequency of use (audio): [Not provided in notes]

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Good

Comment:

Thomas was attentive and followed instructions when engaged, showing understanding of vocabulary such as moods, genders, and weather. He participated in activities like pairing numbers with spots and repeating phrases from the story "Why are you so sad." However, he was not overly participative and required encouragement to join group routines. He enjoyed the "spots and stripes" activity and story time but showed some uncooperative moments, such as refusing to high-five the teacher after repeated reminders about handling materials.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Good

Comment:

Thomas demonstrated a solid grasp of vocabulary and structures, including moods, genders, and weather. He could count very well and pair numbers with spots. While he needed occasional help to recall full structures or identify animals, he showed improvement in repeating phrases and following instructions. His participation in the "Let's go to the zoo" activity was noted, though he did not perform the gestures like the others.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Good

Comment:

Thomas generally treated classmates and the teacher with respect but showed moments of uncooperativeness, such as refusing to high-five after repeated reminders. He took care of materials when reminded but initially struggled to follow routines without prompting. His behaviour improved when engaged in activities he enjoyed, such as story time and the "spots and stripes" game.

Marks

Oral Test Mark: [Not provided in notes]

Final comment

Thomas showed progress in his learning this term, particularly in vocabulary recall and participation in structured activities. While he required occasional encouragement to fully engage, his enthusiasm for story time and certain games was evident. Continued practice with the audio tracks would help reinforce the vocabulary and structures learned this year.

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Thomas

Participation/Motivation

Comment:

Learning process

Comment:

Thomas could show that he understood the vocabulary. He could tell the moods, the genders and the weather, and he could do the gestures for the weather. Behaviour

Comment:

Thomas was a bit uncooperative at times. He had to be carried to the group for the routines, and he refused to highfive the teacher after the cards were taken.

Final comment

Thomas has shown that he can understand the vocabulary and repeat the phrases very well. He can count very well and do the test very well. He also enjoys story time and the activities, although he was not overly participative at times and had to be carried to the group for the routines.

Kids&Us

Objectives and content of the Sam course, 3rd term

Term report (3rd term)

Data

  • Name: Thomas
  • Teacher: Clarisse Bydens
  • Level: Sam Saturday 2025/2026

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Good

Comment:
Thomas usually came to class in a good mood and showed interest in the activities. He particularly seemed to enjoy the "Let's go to the zoo" activity, the "Why are you so sad?" story and the routines. However, he was sometimes a bit uncooperative and needed reminders to stay involved and participate with the group. At times, he had to be encouraged to join in with the class, but when focused, he was able to follow along well.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Good

Comment:
Thomas's English knowledge is good. He can understand and repeat the vocabulary and structures worked on in class. He was able to tell the colours, the genders and the moods, and he could also repeat the weather and do the gestures for it. He could count very well and matched the dot cards with the correct numbers successfully. He sometimes needed a little help to remember the full structure, but he showed a good understanding of the content and could respond well when supported.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Acceptable

Comment:
Thomas is a quiet student who can be attentive and cooperative when he is engaged in the lesson. He sometimes needed to be accompanied more closely into the activities and was not always willing to participate with the group. On one occasion, he did not respect the classroom materials and needed several reminders to handle them properly. Nevertheless, he was able to show his understanding during the activities and, when focused, he worked well with the teacher and the class.

Marks

Oral Test Mark: 9.00 Very good. Thomas could do the test very well and showed a good understanding of the vocabulary and structures.

Final comment

Throughout the term, Thomas has shown that he can understand and use the language well, especially when he is focused and willing to take part. He has made good progress in his oral comprehension and in his ability to repeat the structures learnt in class. It would be beneficial to continue listening to the audio track at home in order to keep in mind the vocabulary words and structures learnt throughout the school year. The teacher would like to encourage Thomas to keep improving his participation and consistency in class.

The report must follow the three-section structure (Participation/Motivation, Learning process, Behaviour) plus a Final comment, as shown in the examples. The notes include absences and mixed behaviour — the report should handle these accurately without inventing positive observations. [{"content":"You are a report card writer for a school teacher. The student notes are the sole source of facts for the report. Every observation, data field, and mark must come from the notes — not from any examples. ## Style & Layout Guide The following are example report cards. Match their tone, voice, vocabulary, section headings, and layout exactly. These examples may differ slightly from the provided additional instructions - in that case, the instructions from the user take precedence. IMPORTANT: Do not copy specific Data field values, Marks, or observations from the examples — only include a Data field or Marks section if that value is present in the student notes. ### Example 1: Informes 3r trimestre Lou-Ellie Colas Acceptable Sam.pdf Kids&Us. Objectives and content of the Sam course, 3rd term --- Term report (3rd term) Data Name: Lou-Ellie Colas Teacher: Clarisse Bydens Level: Sam 2024/2025 Absences: 1 Frequency of use (audio): Pass Participation/Motivation Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Good Comment: Lou-Ellie usually comes to class in a happy, easy-going mood. She takes part in most activities with great enthusiasm. She loves story time and even plays an active role turning the pages of the storybook and sometimes recites the story with the teacher. She also enjoys the "Ants/Bugs counting", the "Have you got Sam's sandwich?", the "Bugs on a picnic" and the "Actions TPR" activities. She is usually quite cooperative with her teacher. Learning process Assessment (Learning process): Good Comment: Lou-Ellie's English knowledge is good. She knows a decent amount of vocabulary and structures. She can sometimes use them in sentences, often requiring the teacher's help to do so. This term, she specifically learnt the different bugs and the different picnic items. She has also reinforced her knowledge of the sizes, the colours, the moods, the types of weather and the genders. She can also count quite well. Behaviour Assessment (Behaviour): Very good Comment: Lou-Ellie is an easy-going, good-humoured, enthusiastic student. She treats the teacher with respect and care. She does sometimes get a bit distracted by external elements and needs to be reminded to stay focused and listen to the teacher. She generally does so without a problem and follows the teacher's instructions very well. She takes good care of the material and gives it back directly when asked. Marks Oral Test Mark: 7.67 Acceptable. --- Final comment Throughout the school year, Lou-Ellie has mostly been coming to class in a cheerful, happy mood. She has been getting better at staying focused during most of the lesson. She has also made progress in her learning, developing both her memory and her comprehension skills. Therefore, it is recommended to listen to the audio track during the summer holidays in order to keep in mind the vocabulary words and structures learnt throughout the school year. ### Example 2: Informes 3r trimestre Lina Carboni Very Good Sam.pdf Kids&Us Objectives and content of the Sam course, 3rd term --- Term report (3rd term) Data Name: Lina Carboni Teacher: Clarisse Bydens Level: Sam 2024/2025 Absences: 1 Frequency of use (audio): Insufficient Participation/Motivation Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Very good Comment: Lina usually comes to class in a happy mood and a huge smile on her face. She loves story time. She also really enjoys the "Bugs on a picnic", the "Actions TPR" and the "Have you got Sam's sandwich?" activities, as well as any activity involving balloons and face paint. She tends to stay focused and motivated all lesson long, seldom asking when it is time for mum and dad. She is very cooperative with her classmates and her teacher. Learning process Assessment (Learning process): Very good Comment: Lina's English knowledge is very good. She can also count very well. She knows a decent amount of vocabulary words and structures. She can sometimes use them in sentences, often requiring the teacher's help to do so. This term, she specifically learnt the different bugs and the different picnic items. She has also reinforced her knowledge of the sizes, the colours, the moods, the types of weather and the genders. Behaviour Assessment (Behaviour): Very good Comment: Lina is a joyful, fun-loving and good-humoured student. She treats her classmates and her teacher with care and respect. She generally listens attentively to the teacher and stays focused during the activities, usually following the teacher's instructions very well. She gives the material back when asked, she shares it with her classmates and she takes good care of it without a problem. Marks Oral Test Mark: 8.80 Very good. --- Final comment At the beginning of the school year, Lina was a quiet student who was always in a good mood, but needed to be encouraged to participate in the activities. Now, she has found her place in her group. She is also very motivated to take part in the various activities. Moreover, she has made great progress in her English learning, developing both her memory and comprehension skills. Therefore, it is recommended to listen to the audio track during the summer holidays, in order to keep in mind the vocabulary words and structures learnt throughout the school year. ### Example 3: Informes 3r trimestre Arthur Motte Good Sam.pdf Kids&Us Objectives and content of the Sam course, 3rd term --- Term report (3rd term) Data Name: Arthur Motte Teacher: Clarisse Bydens Level: Sam 2024/2025 Absences: 5 Frequency of use (audio): Insufficient Participation/Motivation Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Very good Comment: Arthur usually comes to class in a quiet, yet happy mood. He really seemed to enjoy the "Bugs on a picnic" and the "Have you got Sam's sandwich?" activities as well as story time. Arthur's participation has continued to improve throughout the term, as he participated more willingly in the activities. He is very cooperative with his classmates and the teacher. He also stays focused all lesson long. Learning process Assessment (Learning process): Good Comment: Arthur's English knowledge is good. He knows a decent amount of vocabulary and structures. He can sometimes use them in sentences, often requiring the teacher's help to do so. This term, he specifically learnt the different bugs and the different picnic items. He has also reinforced his knowledge of the sizes, the colours, the moods, the types of weather and the genders. Moreover, Arthur can count quite well. Behaviour Assessment (Behaviour): Very good Comment: Arthur is a reserved, gentle, pleasant, friendly student. He treats his classmates and his teacher with care and respect. Arthur really seems to feel comfortable in his group. He sometimes tries to copy his classmates' lively behaviour, whilst still listening to the teacher and staying focused on the lesson, when reminded to do so. He always treats the material with care, shares it with his classmates and gives it back to the teacher directly when asked. Marks Oral Test Mark: 7.80 Good. --- Final comment Arthur took a while before he felt comfortable in his new group. He slowly came out of his shell and has now developed friendly bonds with his classmates. Moreover, Arthur's English learning and his oral test results have kept improving too. Well done, Arthur! It is recommended to listen to the audio track during the summer holidays, in order to keep in mind the vocabulary words and structures learnt during the school year. ## Student Notes (source of truth — all report content must derive from these) Student: Thomas, Class: Sam Saturday - 2026-05-14: Thomas came in my class today with Jamie, Nicola, Meilin and Élise. Thomas and Meilin were very attentive and did what they were asked, and it was great. Nicola, Thomas and Thomas could really show that they understood the vocabulary, and I was really proud of their work. They could all tell their moods, their genders, and they could also tell the weather. I was really proud, and they could do the gestures for the weather, which was great. - 2026-05-14: The group was a bit tired. Thomas could do the test very well. He did the "Let's go to the zoo" activity, but not with the gestures like the others. - 2026-05-14: (Thomas was not there). They all loved the 'Why are you so sad' story, and they could repeat the phrases very well. - 2026-05-14: It was the day of the test. Thomas was not there. - 2026-05-14: Thomas could not tell the animal but could tell the colour, and he needed help to tell me the animal. He could tell the genders and the moods, needed a bit of help to remember the full structure at times, could pair the numbers with the spots, could count very well, could repeat the weather and do the gestures, and could repeat the full phrase for "Why are you so sad." He was not overly participative, and I had to carry him to the group for the routines. He seemed to love the activity. - 2026-05-14: My Sam Saturday students were rather enthusiastic. Thomas was a bit uncooperative at times. They all could pair up their dot cards with the right number. Apart from Liam, they all could tell the animal on their cards, they all could count well. Hasham did need a little help to guess his animal and count. They all loved the spots and stripes version 2 activity. Apart from Thomas, they all loved the Let's go to the zoo. I had to tell them not to bend the cards and after several remarks, I told them that I was very angry and took the cards from some of them. Thomas and Marius refused to highfive me after but the others were okay. They all enjoyed story time. ## Task Write a report card narrative for this student based on the notes above. response.output:
Kids&Us.

Objectives and content of the Sam course, 3rd term

---

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Thomas

Class: Sam Saturday

Participation/Motivation

Comment:

Thomas was attentive and did what he was asked. He did the "Let's go to the zoo" activity, but not with the gestures like the others. He seemed to love the activity and enjoyed story time. At times, he was not overly participative and had to be carried to the group for the routines.

Learning process

Comment:

Thomas could show that he understood the vocabulary. He could tell the moods, the genders and the weather, and he could do the gestures for the weather. He could also pair the numbers with the spots and count very well. He could repeat the weather and the full phrase for "Why are you so sad." He could tell the colour, but needed help to tell the animal and to remember the full structure at times. He could do the test very well.

Behaviour

Comment:

Thomas was a bit uncooperative at times. He had to be carried to the group for the routines, and he refused to highfive the teacher after the cards were taken.

Final comment

Thomas has shown that he can understand the vocabulary and repeat the phrases very well. He can count very well and do the test very well. He also enjoys story time and the activities, although he was not overly participative at times and had to be carried to the group for the routines.

Objectives and content of the Sam course, 3rd term

---

Term report (3rd term)

Data

  • Name: Thomas
  • Level: Sam Saturday

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Good

Comment:

Thomas's participation varies. He is not always overly participative and can be uncooperative at times, requiring encouragement to engage with group activities. He needed assistance to join routines initially. However, he does participate in class activities and shows enthusiasm for certain tasks such as the 'Let's go to the zoo' activity and story time.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Good

Comment:

Thomas shows good progress in his English knowledge. He can tell moods, genders, and weather, and perform gestures for weather. He can count very well and pair numbers with spots. He can repeat phrases from stories and perform well in tests. However, he sometimes needs help with animal vocabulary and remembering full sentence structures.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Good

Comment:

Thomas generally maintains good behaviour in class. He follows instructions when reminded and takes part in classroom routines. However, he can be uncooperative at times and refused to highfive the teacher after activities, unlike his peers.

Final comment

Throughout the term, Thomas has shown improvement in his English skills, particularly in vocabulary, counting, and understanding weather-related vocabulary and gestures. He performs well in tests and enjoys activities like story time and 'Let's go to the zoo'. While he sometimes needs encouragement to participate fully and can display uncooperative behaviour, he is attentive when engaged and has demonstrated solid progress in his learning.

Kids&Us

Objectives and content of the Sam course, 3rd term

Term report (3rd term)

Data

  • Name: Thomas
  • Teacher: Clarisse Bydens
  • Level: Sam Saturday 2025/2026

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Good

Comment:
Thomas usually came to class in a good mood and showed interest in the activities. He particularly seemed to enjoy the "Let's go to the zoo" activity, the "Why are you so sad?" story and the routines. However, he was sometimes a bit uncooperative and needed reminders to stay involved and participate with the group. At times, he had to be encouraged to join in with the class, but when focused, he was able to follow along well.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Good

Comment:
Thomas's English knowledge is good. He can understand and repeat the vocabulary and structures worked on in class. He was able to tell the colours, the genders and the moods, and he could also repeat the weather and do the gestures for it. He could count very well and matched the dot cards with the correct numbers successfully. He sometimes needed a little help to remember the full structure, but he showed a good understanding of the content and could respond well when supported.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Acceptable

Comment:
Thomas is a quiet student who can be attentive and cooperative when he is engaged in the lesson. He sometimes needed to be accompanied more closely into the activities and was not always willing to participate with the group. On one occasion, he did not respect the classroom materials and needed several reminders to handle them properly. Nevertheless, he was able to show his understanding during the activities and, when focused, he worked well with the teacher and the class.

Marks

Oral Test Mark: 9.00 Very good. Thomas could do the test very well and showed a good understanding of the vocabulary and structures.

Final comment

Throughout the term, Thomas has shown that he can understand and use the language well, especially when he is focused and willing to take part. He has made good progress in his oral comprehension and in his ability to repeat the structures learnt in class. It would be beneficial to continue listening to the audio track at home in order to keep in mind the vocabulary words and structures learnt throughout the school year. The teacher would like to encourage Thomas to keep improving his participation and consistency in class.

The report must follow the three-section structure (Participation/Motivation, Learning process, Behaviour) plus a Final comment, as shown in the examples. The notes include absences and mixed behaviour — the report should handle these accurately without inventing positive observations. Score 1-5 on: - structure: three sections + final comment, each with Assessment and Comment - grounding: every statement traceable to notes (no invented observations) - absences: absent sessions noted correctly, not fabricated into positive observations - tone: warm but honest, matches examples "}]

Kids&Us

Objectives and content of the Sam course, 3rd term

Term report (3rd term)

Data

  • Name: Thomas
  • Teacher: Clarisse Bydens
  • Level: Sam Saturday 2025/2026

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Good

Comment:
Thomas usually came to class in a good mood and showed interest in the activities. He particularly seemed to enjoy the "Let's go to the zoo" activity, the "Why are you so sad?" story and the routines. However, he was sometimes a bit uncooperative and needed reminders to stay involved and participate with the group. At times, he had to be encouraged to join in with the class, but when focused, he was able to follow along well.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Good

Comment:
Thomas's English knowledge is good. He can understand and repeat the vocabulary and structures worked on in class. He was able to tell the colours, the genders and the moods, and he could also repeat the weather and do the gestures for it. He could count very well and matched the dot cards with the correct numbers successfully. He sometimes needed a little help to remember the full structure, but he showed a good understanding of the content and could respond well when supported.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Acceptable

Comment:
Thomas is a quiet student who can be attentive and cooperative when he is engaged in the lesson. He sometimes needed to be accompanied more closely into the activities and was not always willing to participate with the group. On one occasion, he did not respect the classroom materials and needed several reminders to handle them properly. Nevertheless, he was able to show his understanding during the activities and, when focused, he worked well with the teacher and the class.

Marks

Oral Test Mark: 9.00 Very good. Thomas could do the test very well and showed a good understanding of the vocabulary and structures.

Final comment

Throughout the term, Thomas has shown that he can understand and use the language well, especially when he is focused and willing to take part. He has made good progress in his oral comprehension and in his ability to repeat the structures learnt in class. It would be beneficial to continue listening to the audio track at home in order to keep in mind the vocabulary words and structures learnt throughout the school year. The teacher would like to encourage Thomas to keep improving his participation and consistency in class.

The report must follow the three-section structure (Participation/Motivation, Learning process, Behaviour) plus a Final comment, as shown in the examples. The notes include absences and mixed behaviour — the report should handle these accurately without inventing positive observations. Score 1-5 on: - structure: three sections + final comment, each with Assessment and Comment - grounding: every statement traceable to notes (no invented observations) - absences: absent sessions noted correctly, not fabricated into positive observations - tone: warm but honest, matches examples

prompt.raw:
[{"content":"You are a report card writer for a school teacher. The student notes are the sole source of facts for the report. Every observation, data field, and mark must come from the notes — not from any examples. ## Style & Layout Guide The following are example report cards. Match their tone, voice, vocabulary, section headings, and layout exactly. These examples may differ slightly from the provided additional instructions - in that case, the instructions from the user take precedence. IMPORTANT: Do not copy specific Data field values, Marks, or observations from the examples — only include a Data field or Marks section if that value is present in the student notes. ### Example 1: Informes 3r trimestre Lou-Ellie Colas Acceptable Sam.pdf Kids&Us. Objectives and content of the Sam course, 3rd term --- Term report (3rd term) Data Name: Lou-Ellie Colas Teacher: Clarisse Bydens Level: Sam 2024/2025 Absences: 1 Frequency of use (audio): Pass Participation/Motivation Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Good Comment: Lou-Ellie usually comes to class in a happy, easy-going mood. She takes part in most activities with great enthusiasm. She loves story time and even plays an active role turning the pages of the storybook and sometimes recites the story with the teacher. She also enjoys the "Ants/Bugs counting", the "Have you got Sam's sandwich?", the "Bugs on a picnic" and the "Actions TPR" activities. She is usually quite cooperative with her teacher. Learning process Assessment (Learning process): Good Comment: Lou-Ellie's English knowledge is good. She knows a decent amount of vocabulary and structures. She can sometimes use them in sentences, often requiring the teacher's help to do so. This term, she specifically learnt the different bugs and the different picnic items. She has also reinforced her knowledge of the sizes, the colours, the moods, the types of weather and the genders. She can also count quite well. Behaviour Assessment (Behaviour): Very good Comment: Lou-Ellie is an easy-going, good-humoured, enthusiastic student. She treats the teacher with respect and care. She does sometimes get a bit distracted by external elements and needs to be reminded to stay focused and listen to the teacher. She generally does so without a problem and follows the teacher's instructions very well. She takes good care of the material and gives it back directly when asked. Marks Oral Test Mark: 7.67 Acceptable. --- Final comment Throughout the school year, Lou-Ellie has mostly been coming to class in a cheerful, happy mood. She has been getting better at staying focused during most of the lesson. She has also made progress in her learning, developing both her memory and her comprehension skills. Therefore, it is recommended to listen to the audio track during the summer holidays in order to keep in mind the vocabulary words and structures learnt throughout the school year. ### Example 2: Informes 3r trimestre Lina Carboni Very Good Sam.pdf Kids&Us Objectives and content of the Sam course, 3rd term --- Term report (3rd term) Data Name: Lina Carboni Teacher: Clarisse Bydens Level: Sam 2024/2025 Absences: 1 Frequency of use (audio): Insufficient Participation/Motivation Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Very good Comment: Lina usually comes to class in a happy mood and a huge smile on her face. She loves story time. She also really enjoys the "Bugs on a picnic", the "Actions TPR" and the "Have you got Sam's sandwich?" activities, as well as any activity involving balloons and face paint. She tends to stay focused and motivated all lesson long, seldom asking when it is time for mum and dad. She is very cooperative with her classmates and her teacher. Learning process Assessment (Learning process): Very good Comment: Lina's English knowledge is very good. She can also count very well. She knows a decent amount of vocabulary words and structures. She can sometimes use them in sentences, often requiring the teacher's help to do so. This term, she specifically learnt the different bugs and the different picnic items. She has also reinforced her knowledge of the sizes, the colours, the moods, the types of weather and the genders. Behaviour Assessment (Behaviour): Very good Comment: Lina is a joyful, fun-loving and good-humoured student. She treats her classmates and her teacher with care and respect. She generally listens attentively to the teacher and stays focused during the activities, usually following the teacher's instructions very well. She gives the material back when asked, she shares it with her classmates and she takes good care of it without a problem. Marks Oral Test Mark: 8.80 Very good. --- Final comment At the beginning of the school year, Lina was a quiet student who was always in a good mood, but needed to be encouraged to participate in the activities. Now, she has found her place in her group. She is also very motivated to take part in the various activities. Moreover, she has made great progress in her English learning, developing both her memory and comprehension skills. Therefore, it is recommended to listen to the audio track during the summer holidays, in order to keep in mind the vocabulary words and structures learnt throughout the school year. ### Example 3: Informes 3r trimestre Arthur Motte Good Sam.pdf Kids&Us Objectives and content of the Sam course, 3rd term --- Term report (3rd term) Data Name: Arthur Motte Teacher: Clarisse Bydens Level: Sam 2024/2025 Absences: 5 Frequency of use (audio): Insufficient Participation/Motivation Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Very good Comment: Arthur usually comes to class in a quiet, yet happy mood. He really seemed to enjoy the "Bugs on a picnic" and the "Have you got Sam's sandwich?" activities as well as story time. Arthur's participation has continued to improve throughout the term, as he participated more willingly in the activities. He is very cooperative with his classmates and the teacher. He also stays focused all lesson long. Learning process Assessment (Learning process): Good Comment: Arthur's English knowledge is good. He knows a decent amount of vocabulary and structures. He can sometimes use them in sentences, often requiring the teacher's help to do so. This term, he specifically learnt the different bugs and the different picnic items. He has also reinforced his knowledge of the sizes, the colours, the moods, the types of weather and the genders. Moreover, Arthur can count quite well. Behaviour Assessment (Behaviour): Very good Comment: Arthur is a reserved, gentle, pleasant, friendly student. He treats his classmates and his teacher with care and respect. Arthur really seems to feel comfortable in his group. He sometimes tries to copy his classmates' lively behaviour, whilst still listening to the teacher and staying focused on the lesson, when reminded to do so. He always treats the material with care, shares it with his classmates and gives it back to the teacher directly when asked. Marks Oral Test Mark: 7.80 Good. --- Final comment Arthur took a while before he felt comfortable in his new group. He slowly came out of his shell and has now developed friendly bonds with his classmates. Moreover, Arthur's English learning and his oral test results have kept improving too. Well done, Arthur! It is recommended to listen to the audio track during the summer holidays, in order to keep in mind the vocabulary words and structures learnt during the school year. ## Student Notes (source of truth — all report content must derive from these) Student: Thomas, Class: Sam Saturday - 2026-05-14: Thomas came in my class today with Jamie, Nicola, Meilin and Élise. Thomas and Meilin were very attentive and did what they were asked, and it was great. Nicola, Thomas and Thomas could really show that they understood the vocabulary, and I was really proud of their work. They could all tell their moods, their genders, and they could also tell the weather. I was really proud, and they could do the gestures for the weather, which was great. - 2026-05-14: The group was a bit tired. Thomas could do the test very well. He did the "Let's go to the zoo" activity, but not with the gestures like the others. - 2026-05-14: (Thomas was not there). They all loved the 'Why are you so sad' story, and they could repeat the phrases very well. - 2026-05-14: It was the day of the test. Thomas was not there. - 2026-05-14: Thomas could not tell the animal but could tell the colour, and he needed help to tell me the animal. He could tell the genders and the moods, needed a bit of help to remember the full structure at times, could pair the numbers with the spots, could count very well, could repeat the weather and do the gestures, and could repeat the full phrase for "Why are you so sad." He was not overly participative, and I had to carry him to the group for the routines. He seemed to love the activity. - 2026-05-14: My Sam Saturday students were rather enthusiastic. Thomas was a bit uncooperative at times. They all could pair up their dot cards with the right number. Apart from Liam, they all could tell the animal on their cards, they all could count well. Hasham did need a little help to guess his animal and count. They all loved the spots and stripes version 2 activity. Apart from Thomas, they all loved the Let's go to the zoo. I had to tell them not to bend the cards and after several remarks, I told them that I was very angry and took the cards from some of them. Thomas and Marius refused to highfive me after but the others were okay. They all enjoyed story time. ## Task Write a report card narrative for this student based on the notes above. Output the report as clean HTML (using

,

,
    ,
  • tags as appropriate). Do not include , , or wrapper tags — just the content HTML. Only include structured Data fields (Absences, Marks, Frequency of use, etc.) if those values are explicitly present in the notes. Do not invent or carry over values from the examples. Every statement in the report must be traceable to the notes. Do not invent observations. Follow the tone, section headings, and layout of the examples exactly. Do not replicate their specific Data field values, Marks, or observations — the report content comes only from the student notes above. ","role":"user"}]

Objectives and content of the Sam course, 3rd term

---

Term report (3rd term)

Data

  • Name: Thomas
  • Level: Sam Saturday

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Good

Comment:

Thomas's participation varies. He is not always overly participative and can be uncooperative at times, requiring encouragement to engage with group activities. He needed assistance to join routines initially. However, he does participate in class activities and shows enthusiasm for certain tasks such as the 'Let's go to the zoo' activity and story time.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Good

Comment:

Thomas shows good progress in his English knowledge. He can tell moods, genders, and weather, and perform gestures for weather. He can count very well and pair numbers with spots. He can repeat phrases from stories and perform well in tests. However, he sometimes needs help with animal vocabulary and remembering full sentence structures.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Good

Comment:

Thomas generally maintains good behaviour in class. He follows instructions when reminded and takes part in classroom routines. However, he can be uncooperative at times and refused to highfive the teacher after activities, unlike his peers.

Final comment

Throughout the term, Thomas has shown improvement in his English skills, particularly in vocabulary, counting, and understanding weather-related vocabulary and gestures. He performs well in tests and enjoys activities like story time and 'Let's go to the zoo'. While he sometimes needs encouragement to participate fully and can display uncooperative behaviour, he is attentive when engaged and has demonstrated solid progress in his learning.

componentResults.reason: The report follows the required three-section structure with Participation/Motivation, Learning process, Behaviour, and a Final comment, and each main section includes an assessment and comment. Most learning and behaviour statements are grounded in the notes, such as Thomas knowing moods/genders/weather, counting well, pairing numbers with spots/dots, needing help with animals/full structures, being uncooperative, being carried to routines, and refusing a high-five after the card incident. However, there are grounding issues: it attributes enjoyment of the 'Why are you so sad' story somewhat strongly despite one note explicitly saying Thomas was absent for that session, and it does not clearly handle the absences. Some wording is also more detailed/negative than the style examples, though the tone remains generally warm and honest. Overall it is structurally solid and mostly traceable, but loses points for absence handling and a few questionable positive attributions.

---

Term report (3rd term)

Data

  • Name: Thomas
  • Class: Sam Saturday

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Acceptable

Comment:
Thomas shows moments of attentiveness and understanding, particularly when working with vocabulary like moods, genders, and weather. He enjoys certain activities, such as the "Why are you so sad" story, where he could repeat phrases well. However, his participation is inconsistent—he can be uncooperative at times and requires encouragement to join group routines. While he engages with activities he likes, such as pairing numbers with spots and counting, he sometimes resists participation, as seen when he refused to high-five the teacher after a reminder about handling materials.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Good

Comment:
Thomas demonstrates a solid grasp of key vocabulary and structures. He can identify and express moods, genders, and weather conditions, including performing the corresponding gestures for weather. His counting skills are strong, and he can pair numbers with visual cues like spots. While he occasionally needs support to recall full sentence structures or identify animals, he shows understanding when guided. This term, he reinforced his knowledge of colours, numbers, and phrases from stories like "Why are you so sad," though his ability to apply gestures in activities like "Let's go to the zoo" was limited compared to his peers.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Acceptable

Comment:
Thomas is generally cooperative when engaged in activities he enjoys, but his behaviour can be inconsistent. He follows instructions well in structured tasks, such as counting or repeating phrases, but sometimes requires physical guidance (e.g., being carried to the group for routines). He struggles with frustration when corrected, as seen when he refused to high-five the teacher after a reprimand about bending cards. While he shares enthusiasm for activities like the "spots and stripes" game, his resistance to participation and occasional defiance affect his overall behaviour in class.

---

Final comment

Thomas has shown clear strengths in vocabulary retention and counting, particularly when the activities capture his interest. His ability to repeat phrases and perform gestures demonstrates good comprehension, though he sometimes needs reminders to stay engaged. To build consistency, it would be beneficial for Thomas to practice routines and gestures more actively, especially in group settings. Encouraging his participation in a positive manner—such as through stories and counting games he enjoys—could help him feel more confident. Keep reinforcing the vocabulary and structures he knows well, and consider reviewing animals and full sentences to solidify his progress. Well done on the areas where you’ve shown strong understanding, Thomas!

Objectives and content of the Sam course, 3rd term

Term report (3rd term)

Data

  • Name: Thomas
  • Teacher: Clarisse Bydens
  • Level: Sam Saturday 2025/2026

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Good

Comment:
Thomas usually came to class in a good mood and showed interest in the activities. Assessment (Learning process): Good

Marks

[{"content":"You are a report card writer for a school teacher. The student notes are the sole source of facts for the report. Every observation, data field, and mark must come from the notes — not from any examples. ## Style & Layout Guide The following are example report cards. Match their tone, voice, vocabulary, section headings, and layout exactly. These examples may differ slightly from the provided additional instructions - in that case, the instructions from the user take precedence. IMPORTANT: Do not copy specific Data field values, Marks, or observations from the examples — only include a Data field or Marks section if that value is present in the student notes. ### Example 1: Informes 3r trimestre Lou-Ellie Colas Acceptable Sam.pdf Kids&Us. Objectives and content of the Sam course, 3rd term --- Term report (3rd term) Data Name: Lou-Ellie Colas Teacher: Clarisse Bydens Level: Sam 2024/2025 Absences: 1 Frequency of use (audio): Pass Participation/Motivation Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Good Comment: Lou-Ellie usually comes to class in a happy, easy-going mood. She takes part in most activities with great enthusiasm. She loves story time and even plays an active role turning the pages of the storybook and sometimes recites the story with the teacher. She also enjoys the "Ants/Bugs counting", the "Have you got Sam's sandwich?", the "Bugs on a picnic" and the "Actions TPR" activities. She is usually quite cooperative with her teacher. Learning process Assessment (Learning process): Good Comment: Lou-Ellie's English knowledge is good. She knows a decent amount of vocabulary and structures. She can sometimes use them in sentences, often requiring the teacher's help to do so. This term, she specifically learnt the different bugs and the different picnic items. She has also reinforced her knowledge of the sizes, the colours, the moods, the types of weather and the genders. She can also count quite well. Behaviour Assessment (Behaviour): Very good Comment: Lou-Ellie is an easy-going, good-humoured, enthusiastic student. She treats the teacher with respect and care. She does sometimes get a bit distracted by external elements and needs to be reminded to stay focused and listen to the teacher. She generally does so without a problem and follows the teacher's instructions very well. She takes good care of the material and gives it back directly when asked. Marks Oral Test Mark: 7.67 Acceptable. --- Final comment Throughout the school year, Lou-Ellie has mostly been coming to class in a cheerful, happy mood. She has been getting better at staying focused during most of the lesson. She has also made progress in her learning, developing both her memory and her comprehension skills. Therefore, it is recommended to listen to the audio track during the summer holidays in order to keep in mind the vocabulary words and structures learnt throughout the school year. ### Example 2: Informes 3r trimestre Lina Carboni Very Good Sam.pdf Kids&Us Objectives and content of the Sam course, 3rd term --- Term report (3rd term) Data Name: Lina Carboni Teacher: Clarisse Bydens Level: Sam 2024/2025 Absences: 1 Frequency of use (audio): Insufficient Participation/Motivation Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Very good Comment: Lina usually comes to class in a happy mood and a huge smile on her face. She tends to stay focused and motivated all lesson long, seldom asking when it is time for mum and dad. She is very cooperative with her classmates and her teacher. Learning process Assessment (Learning process): Very good Comment: Lina's English knowledge is very good. She can also count very well. She knows a decent amount of vocabulary words and structures. She can sometimes use them in sentences, often requiring the teacher's help to do so. This term, she specifically learnt the different bugs and the different picnic items. She has also reinforced her knowledge of the sizes, the colours, the moods, the types of weather and the genders. Behaviour Assessment (Behaviour): Very good Comment: Lina is a joyful, fun-loving and good-humoured student. She treats her classmates and her teacher with care and respect. She generally listens attentively to the teacher and stays focused during the activities, usually following the teacher's instructions very well. She gives the material back when asked, she shares it with her classmates and she takes good care of it without a problem. Marks Oral Test Mark: 8.80 Very good. --- Final comment At the beginning of the school year, Lina was a quiet student who was always in a good mood, but needed to be encouraged to participate in the activities. Now, she has found her place in her group. She is also very motivated to take part in the various activities. Moreover, she has made great progress in her English learning, developing both her memory and comprehension skills. Therefore, it is recommended to listen to the audio track during the summer holidays, in order to keep in mind the vocabulary words and structures learnt throughout the school year. ### Example 3: Informes 3r trimestre Arthur Motte Good Sam.pdf Kids&Us Objectives and content of the Sam course, 3rd term --- Term report (3rd term) Data Name: Arthur Motte Teacher: Clarisse Bydens Level: Sam 2024/2025 Absences: 5 Frequency of use (audio): Insufficient Participation/Motivation Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Very good Comment: Arthur usually comes to class in a quiet, yet happy mood. He really seemed to enjoy the "Bugs on a picnic" and the "Have you got Sam's sandwich?" activities as well as story time. Arthur's participation has continued to improve throughout the term, as he participated more willingly in the activities. He is very cooperative with his classmates and the teacher. He also stays focused all lesson long. Learning process Assessment (Learning process): Good Comment: Arthur's English knowledge is good. He knows a decent amount of vocabulary and structures. He can sometimes use them in sentences, often requiring the teacher's help to do so. This term, he specifically learnt the different bugs and the different picnic items. He has also reinforced his knowledge of the sizes, the colours, the moods, the types of weather and the genders. Moreover, Arthur can count quite well. Behaviour Assessment (Behaviour): Very good Comment: Arthur is a reserved, gentle, pleasant, friendly student. He treats his classmates and his teacher with care and respect. Arthur really seems to feel comfortable in his group. He sometimes tries to copy his classmates' lively behaviour, whilst still listening to the teacher and staying focused on the lesson, when reminded to do so. He always treats the material with care, shares it with his classmates and gives it back to the teacher directly when asked. Marks Oral Test Mark: 7.80 Good. --- Final comment Arthur took a while before he felt comfortable in his new group. He slowly came out of his shell and has now developed friendly bonds with his classmates. Moreover, Arthur's English learning and his oral test results have kept improving too. Well done, Arthur! He was not overly participative, and I had to carry him to the group for the routines. He seemed to love the activity. - 2026-05-14: My Sam Saturday students were rather enthusiastic. response.output:
```html

Kids&Us

Objectives and content of the Sam course, 3rd term

---

Term report (3rd term)

Data

  • Name: Thomas
  • Class: Sam Saturday

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Acceptable

Comment:
Thomas shows moments of attentiveness and understanding, particularly when working with vocabulary like moods, genders, and weather. He enjoys certain activities, such as the "Why are you so sad" story, where he could repeat phrases well. However, his participation is inconsistent—he can be uncooperative at times and requires encouragement to join group routines. While he engages with activities he likes, such as pairing numbers with spots and counting, he sometimes resists participation, as seen when he refused to high-five the teacher after a reminder about handling materials.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Good

Comment:
Thomas demonstrates a solid grasp of key vocabulary and structures. He can identify and express moods, genders, and weather conditions, including performing the corresponding gestures for weather. His counting skills are strong, and he can pair numbers with visual cues like spots. While he occasionally needs support to recall full sentence structures or identify animals, he shows understanding when guided. This term, he reinforced his knowledge of colours, numbers, and phrases from stories like "Why are you so sad," though his ability to apply gestures in activities like "Let's go to the zoo" was limited compared to his peers.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Acceptable

Comment:
Thomas is generally cooperative when engaged in activities he enjoys, but his behaviour can be inconsistent. He follows instructions well in structured tasks, such as counting or repeating phrases, but sometimes requires physical guidance (e.g., being carried to the group for routines). He struggles with frustration when corrected, as seen when he refused to high-five the teacher after a reprimand about bending cards. While he shares enthusiasm for activities like the "spots and stripes" game, his resistance to participation and occasional defiance affect his overall behaviour in class.

---

Final comment

Thomas has shown clear strengths in vocabulary retention and counting, particularly when the activities capture his interest. His ability to repeat phrases and perform gestures demonstrates good comprehension, though he sometimes needs reminders to stay engaged. To build consistency, it would be beneficial for Thomas to practice routines and gestures more actively, especially in group settings. Encouraging his participation in a positive manner—such as through stories and counting games he enjoys—could help him feel more confident. Keep reinforcing the vocabulary and structures he knows well, and consider reviewing animals and full sentences to solidify his progress. Well done on the areas where you’ve shown strong understanding, Thomas!

```

componentResults.reason: The report follows the required three-section structure with Participation/Motivation, Learning process, Behaviour, and a Final comment, and each main section includes an assessment and comment. Most statements are traceable to the notes, including Thomas's good vocabulary understanding, counting, weather/gender/mood knowledge, mixed participation, uncooperative moments, card-handling reminders, refusal to high-five, and 2 absences. However, there are some quality issues: the teacher field is blank, the level is written imprecisely as 'Sam Saturday 2026' rather than the fuller school-year style, the phrase 'sometimes needed to be carried' overgeneralizes a single note and sounds too harsh for the style, and saying he loved the 'Why are you so sad' story is slightly risky because one source note about that story explicitly says Thomas was absent, though another note suggests he enjoyed an activity related to it. Tone is honest but a bit less polished and warm than the examples. Overall it is mostly accurate and structured, with minor grounding and style concerns.

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Acceptable

Comment:
Thomas was not overly participative and sometimes needed to be carried to the group for routines. He showed enthusiasm for some activities, such as the "Why are you so sad" story, which he loved. However, he was a bit uncooperative at times, particularly during the "Let's go to the zoo" activity, where he did not perform the gestures like the others. He also refused to high-five the teacher after a reminder about handling the cards properly.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Good

Comment:
Thomas demonstrated a good understanding of vocabulary and structures. He could tell his moods, genders, and the weather, and he could repeat the full phrase for "Why are you so sad." He could also count very well and pair numbers with spots. He needed help to remember the full structure at times and could not always tell the animal but could identify the colour. He performed well on the test.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Acceptable

Comment:
Thomas was attentive and did what he was asked, but he was sometimes uncooperative. He needed reminders to follow routines and handle materials properly. After several remarks about bending the cards, he refused to high-five the teacher, showing some resistance.

---

Final comment

Thomas has shown a good understanding of the vocabulary and structures taught this term, particularly in identifying moods, genders, weather, and counting. However, his participation and cooperation could improve, as he sometimes resisted joining group activities or following instructions. It is recommended to continue practicing the vocabulary and structures to reinforce his learning.

Kids&Us

Objectives and content of the Sam course, 3rd term

Term report (3rd term)

Data

  • Name: Thomas
  • Teacher: Clarisse Bydens
  • Level: Sam Saturday 2025/2026

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Good

Comment:
Thomas usually came to class in a good mood and showed interest in the activities. He particularly seemed to enjoy the "Let's go to the zoo" activity, the "Why are you so sad?" story and the routines. However, he was sometimes a bit uncooperative and needed reminders to stay involved and participate with the group. At times, he had to be encouraged to join in with the class, but when focused, he was able to follow along well.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Good

Comment:
Thomas's English knowledge is good. He can understand and repeat the vocabulary and structures worked on in class. He was able to tell the colours, the genders and the moods, and he could also repeat the weather and do the gestures for it. He could count very well and matched the dot cards with the correct numbers successfully. He sometimes needed a little help to remember the full structure, but he showed a good understanding of the content and could respond well when supported.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Acceptable

Comment:
Thomas is a quiet student who can be attentive and cooperative when he is engaged in the lesson. He sometimes needed to be accompanied more closely into the activities and was not always willing to participate with the group. On one occasion, he did not respect the classroom materials and needed several reminders to handle them properly. Nevertheless, he was able to show his understanding during the activities and, when focused, he worked well with the teacher and the class.

Marks

Oral Test Mark: 9.00 Very good. Thomas could do the test very well and showed a good understanding of the vocabulary and structures.

Final comment

Throughout the term, Thomas has shown that he can understand and use the language well, especially when he is focused and willing to take part. He has made good progress in his oral comprehension and in his ability to repeat the structures learnt in class. It would be beneficial to continue listening to the audio track at home in order to keep in mind the vocabulary words and structures learnt throughout the school year. The teacher would like to encourage Thomas to keep improving his participation and consistency in class.

The report must follow the three-section structure (Participation/Motivation, Learning process, Behaviour) plus a Final comment, as shown in the examples. The notes include absences and mixed behaviour — the report should handle these accurately without inventing positive observations. Score 1-5 on: - structure: three sections + final comment, each with Assessment and Comment - grounding: every statement traceable to notes (no invented observations) - absences: absent sessions noted correctly, not fabricated into positive observations - tone: warm but honest, matches examples "}]

Kids&Us.

Objectives and content of the Sam course, 3rd term

---

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Thomas
Teacher:
Level: Sam Saturday 2026
Absences: 2

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Acceptable

Comment:
Thomas was not overly participative and sometimes needed to be carried to the group for routines. He showed enthusiasm for some activities, such as the "Why are you so sad" story, which he loved. However, he was a bit uncooperative at times, particularly during the "Let's go to the zoo" activity, where he did not perform the gestures like the others. He also refused to high-five the teacher after a reminder about handling the cards properly.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Good

Comment:
Thomas demonstrated a good understanding of vocabulary and structures. He could tell his moods, genders, and the weather, and he could repeat the full phrase for "Why are you so sad." He could also count very well and pair numbers with spots. He needed help to remember the full structure at times and could not always tell the animal but could identify the colour. He performed well on the test.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Acceptable

Comment:
Thomas was attentive and did what he was asked, but he was sometimes uncooperative. He needed reminders to follow routines and handle materials properly. After several remarks about bending the cards, he refused to high-five the teacher, showing some resistance.

---

Final comment

Thomas has shown a good understanding of the vocabulary and structures taught this term, particularly in identifying moods, genders, weather, and counting. However, his participation and cooperation could improve, as he sometimes resisted joining group activities or following instructions. It is recommended to continue practicing the vocabulary and structures to reinforce his learning.

COURSE OBJECTIVES - FAIRY TALES 2, 3rd term

---

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Emma
Teacher: Huruv Osmanova
Level: Fairy Tales 2 Tuesday & Friday
Absences: 0

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Good
Comment: Emma maintains a polite and attentive demeanour in class. She is respectful toward her peers and teacher, and she actively participates in discussions when encouraged. She asks for vocabulary support in French when needed but adapts by using English when prompted. While she occasionally appears uneasy or defensive, particularly around writing tasks, she responds positively to encouragement and additional time to process questions.

Work

Assessment (Work): Good
Comment: Emma demonstrates commitment to her learning and contributes to group activities. She engages thoughtfully in tasks, revises grammatical structures with reminders, and uses target language effectively in gapped sentences and collaborative exercises. She shows improvement in forming questions and answers using structures such as "would rather…than…" and "prefer…to…". However, she sometimes avoids speaking unless directly asked and occasionally expresses reluctance, particularly during individual writing tasks. She benefits from peer support, such as from Romain and Helena, for vocabulary and spelling assistance.

Performance

Assessment (Performance): Good
Comment: Emma’s performance reflects steady progress in comprehension and expression. She reads aloud with strong pronunciation overall, though she occasionally makes minor errors. She understands and uses new vocabulary well and can summarize chapters using target language provided on the board. She demonstrates confidence in listening and speaking activities, though she sometimes hesitates or makes tense or pronoun errors. She successfully applies grammatical forms such as the causative ("get something done") and comparative structures. Her written work shows attention to detail, though she occasionally struggles with punctuation and capitalization.

---

Final comment

Emma has shown consistent effort and growing confidence this term. She participates actively when supported and responds well to encouragement, particularly when given time to process or when tasks are structured collaboratively. While she occasionally relies on peers or requests translations, she adapts and uses English when prompted. Her reading and oral comprehension are strong, and she applies grammatical structures with increasing accuracy. With continued practice in speaking without hesitation and refining written accuracy, Emma is well on her way to further improvement.
