COURSE OBJECTIVES - FAIRY TALES 2, 3rd term

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Emma
Teacher: Huryv Osmanova
Level: Fairy Tales 2 Tuesday & Friday 2025/2026
Absences: 0

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Excellent

Emma is a polite and well-mannered student who generally shows a positive attitude in class. She is calm, respectful, and attentive, and she works well with both her teacher and classmates. At times, she can be a little hesitant to speak up, but with encouragement she is willing to try and becomes more confident. She responds well when given time to think and can be very receptive to guidance. Her kind and cooperative nature contributes positively to the classroom atmosphere.

Work

Assessment (Work): Good

Emma takes part in class activities and usually completes the work she is asked to do with care. She has shown good effort throughout the term, especially when speaking and working with familiar structures. She can make sentences using the target language and is able to follow the lesson content well. However, she sometimes needs encouragement to try more independently and to avoid relying too much on French. Continuing to build confidence and expanding her vocabulary will help her participate more freely and consistently.

Performance

Assessment (Performance): Good

Emma has made good progress this term. She understands the new vocabulary well and can recognise and use many of the structures covered in class. She is also able to read the stories and chapters well, and her reading is often impressive. In oral activities, she can answer using full structures and usually corrects herself when she makes mistakes, which is very positive. She still needs to work on pronunciation, connectors, and some grammar points such as the third person singular, pronouns, and tense accuracy. With regular practice and more confidence, she will continue to improve.

Marks

Oral Test Mark: 7.10 Good.

Emma performs well orally when she feels comfortable and has enough time to think. She can repeat and use the target language accurately in many situations, and her pronunciation is generally improving. However, she still makes occasional pronunciation mistakes and sometimes needs support to expand her answers.

Writing Mark: 6.80 Good.

Emma's written work shows a good understanding of the language covered this term. She can complete grammar and vocabulary tasks quite well. However, she still makes some errors with capital letters, punctuation, and grammar when writing independently. Further practice with writing and careful revision before tests will help her gain more confidence and accuracy.

Homework mark: 6.50 Acceptable.

Emma has completed some homework and has generally shown a reasonable effort. To improve further, she should make a more consistent effort to practise at home, revise vocabulary regularly, and review the grammar points covered in class.

Final comment

Over the course of the term, Emma has shown a positive attitude and has made steady progress in class. She is polite, attentive, and increasingly willing to try, especially when she is given a bit of time and support. Her reading skills are particularly strong, and she shows good understanding of the content covered in class. To continue improving, she should focus on speaking more confidently, using English as much as possible, and revising vocabulary and grammar regularly. With continued encouragement and practice, Emma is well placed to make further progress next term.

The report must follow the three-section structure (Behaviour, Work, Performance) plus Marks and a Final comment, as shown in the examples. Each section should include an Assessment rating and a Comment paragraph. All observations must be grounded in the source notes. Score 1-5 on: - structure: three sections (Behaviour/Work/Performance) + Marks + Final comment - grounding: every statement traceable to notes (no invented observations) - tone: constructive and honest, matches examples - balance: weaknesses (pronunciation errors, reliance on French, avoiding speaking) acknowledged alongside strengths "}]

COURSE OBJECTIVES - FAIRY TALES 2, 3rd term

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Emma
Teacher: Huryv Osmanova
Level: Fairy Tales 2 Tuesday & Friday 2025/2026
Absences: 0

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Excellent

Emma is a polite and well-mannered student who generally shows a positive attitude in class. She is calm, respectful, and attentive, and she works well with both her teacher and classmates. At times, she can be a little hesitant to speak up, but with encouragement she is willing to try and becomes more confident. She responds well when given time to think and can be very receptive to guidance. Her kind and cooperative nature contributes positively to the classroom atmosphere.

Work

Assessment (Work): Good

Emma takes part in class activities and usually completes the work she is asked to do with care. She has shown good effort throughout the term, especially when speaking and working with familiar structures. She can make sentences using the target language and is able to follow the lesson content well. However, she sometimes needs encouragement to try more independently and to avoid relying too much on French. Continuing to build confidence and expanding her vocabulary will help her participate more freely and consistently.

Performance

Assessment (Performance): Good

Emma has made good progress this term. She understands the new vocabulary well and can recognise and use many of the structures covered in class. She is also able to read the stories and chapters well, and her reading is often impressive. In oral activities, she can answer using full structures and usually corrects herself when she makes mistakes, which is very positive. She still needs to work on pronunciation, connectors, and some grammar points such as the third person singular, pronouns, and tense accuracy. With regular practice and more confidence, she will continue to improve.

Marks

Oral Test Mark: 7.10 Good.

Emma performs well orally when she feels comfortable and has enough time to think. She can repeat and use the target language accurately in many situations, and her pronunciation is generally improving. However, she still makes occasional pronunciation mistakes and sometimes needs support to expand her answers.

Writing Mark: 6.80 Good.

Emma's written work shows a good understanding of the language covered this term. She can complete grammar and vocabulary tasks quite well. However, she still makes some errors with capital letters, punctuation, and grammar when writing independently. Further practice with writing and careful revision before tests will help her gain more confidence and accuracy.

Homework mark: 6.50 Acceptable.

Emma has completed some homework and has generally shown a reasonable effort. To improve further, she should make a more consistent effort to practise at home, revise vocabulary regularly, and review the grammar points covered in class.

Final comment

Over the course of the term, Emma has shown a positive attitude and has made steady progress in class. She is polite, attentive, and increasingly willing to try, especially when she is given a bit of time and support. Her reading skills are particularly strong, and she shows good understanding of the content covered in class. To continue improving, she should focus on speaking more confidently, using English as much as possible, and revising vocabulary and grammar regularly. With continued encouragement and practice, Emma is well placed to make further progress next term.

The report must follow the three-section structure (Behaviour, Work, Performance) plus Marks and a Final comment, as shown in the examples. Each section should include an Assessment rating and a Comment paragraph. All observations must be grounded in the source notes. Score 1-5 on: - structure: three sections (Behaviour/Work/Performance) + Marks + Final comment - grounding: every statement traceable to notes (no invented observations) - tone: constructive and honest, matches examples - balance: weaknesses (pronunciation errors, reliance on French, avoiding speaking) acknowledged alongside strengths

Every observation, data field, and mark must come from the notes — not from any examples. ## Style & Layout Guide The following are example report cards. Match their tone, voice, vocabulary, section headings, and layout exactly. These examples may differ slightly from the provided additional instructions - in that case, the instructions from the user take precedence. IMPORTANT: Do not copy specific Data field values, Marks, or observations from the examples — only include a Data field or Marks section if that value is present in the student notes. ### Example 1: Informes 3r trimestre Alice Ling Excellent.pdf Kids&Us. She is always polite and considerate towards both her classmates and teachers. She actively listens, engages in class discussions, and consistently asks for clarification when she doesn't understand something. Her positive and cooperative nature contributes to a welcoming and supportive classroom environment. Work Assessment (Work): Excellent Comment: Alice remains an engaged and hardworking student. She participates actively in class and completes her tasks with enthusiasm. She consistently takes initiative in her learning and demonstrates great responsibility. However, she should make an effort to revise more regularly and complete all assigned homework to reinforce her understanding of the material. Performance Assessment (Performance): Excellent Comment: Alice continues to excel in her academic performance. She has achieved excellent results in her assessments and demonstrates a strong grasp of vocabulary, grammar, and comprehension. Her test results are consistently impressive, and she shows great confidence in both oral and written tasks. Marks Oral Test Mark: 10.00 Excellent. Writing Mark: 9.80 Excellent. Homework mark: 0.00 Needs to improve. --- Final comment Alice has proven to be a bright, motivated, and dedicated student. She consistently demonstrates a strong command of English and maintains excellent behaviour and participation in class. With improved consistency in completing homework and regular revision, she is well-positioned to continue excelling. ### Example 2: Informes 3r trimestre Romain Fafchamps Good.pdf Kids&Us. COURSE OBJECTIVES - FAIRY TALES 1, 3rd term --- Term report (3rd term) Data Name: Romain Fachamps Teacher: Huryu Osmanova Level: Tweens 3 2024/2025 Absences: 0 Behaviour Assessment (Behaviour): Excellent Comment: Romain continues to be a kind, respectful, and responsible student. With improved homework habits and continued focus on grammar and vocabulary, she has great potential to reach excellence across all areas. ## Student Notes (source of truth — all report content must derive from these) Student: Emma, Class: Fairy Tales 2 Tuesday & Friday - 2026-05-14: Emma came very late, 25 minutes late, and she left early. She was in a rather good mood, but she didn't understand some of the vocabulary, so she asked me to tell some of the words in French. She looked a bit desperate because she didn't understand what was going on, so she was a bit upset about that. When she saw that it was a writing task, she looked very happy not to have to do it because she was leaving at that time. She still sometimes makes errors when she pronounces some words, but I told her I found her very good today and that I was very happy and proud of her. - 2026-05-14: Emma could use some of the grammatical structures and remembered a little bit of the previous chapters. She also sometimes makes mistakes when she speaks, especially with pronouns, and sometimes forgets tenses or the third-person singular -s. - 2026-05-14: Helena helped her a lot with the vocabulary. When we worked collaboratively, Emma participated, but when it was individual, she asked to go to the loo and stayed there a bit. Emma and Romain needed a bit of help for inspiration, and Emma sometimes needed a bit of help with pronunciation. She knew how to use the connector but had to be reminded of its purpose. - 2026-05-14: Emma made some efforts to talk a bit more and expand her answers, being more confident. response.output:
COURSE OBJECTIVES - FAIRY TALES 2, 3rd term

---

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Emma
Teacher: Huruv Osmanova
Level: Fairy Tales 2 Tuesday & Friday
Absences: 0

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Good
Comment: Emma maintains a polite and attentive demeanour in class. She is respectful toward her peers and teacher, and she actively participates in discussions when encouraged. She asks for vocabulary support in French when needed but adapts by using English when prompted. While she occasionally appears uneasy or defensive, particularly around writing tasks, she responds positively to encouragement and additional time to process questions.

Work

Assessment (Work): Good
Comment: Emma demonstrates commitment to her learning and contributes to group activities. She engages thoughtfully in tasks, revises grammatical structures with reminders, and uses target language effectively in gapped sentences and collaborative exercises. She shows improvement in forming questions and answers using structures such as "would rather…than…" and "prefer…to…". However, she sometimes avoids speaking unless directly asked and occasionally expresses reluctance, particularly during individual writing tasks. She benefits from peer support, such as from Romain and Helena, for vocabulary and spelling assistance.

Performance

Assessment (Performance): Good
Comment: Emma’s performance reflects steady progress in comprehension and expression. She reads aloud with strong pronunciation overall, though she occasionally makes minor errors. She understands and uses new vocabulary well and can summarize chapters using target language provided on the board. She demonstrates confidence in listening and speaking activities, though she sometimes hesitates or makes tense or pronoun errors. She successfully applies grammatical forms such as the causative ("get something done") and comparative structures. Her written work shows attention to detail, though she occasionally struggles with punctuation and capitalization.

---

Final comment

Emma has shown consistent effort and growing confidence this term. She participates actively when supported and responds well to encouragement, particularly when given time to process or when tasks are structured collaboratively. While she occasionally relies on peers or requests translations, she adapts and uses English when prompted. Her reading and oral comprehension are strong, and she applies grammatical structures with increasing accuracy. With continued practice in speaking without hesitation and refining written accuracy, Emma is well on her way to further improvement.

COURSE OBJECTIVES - FAIRY TALES 2, Tuesday & Friday

---

Term report

Data

  • Name: Emma
  • Class: Fairy Tales 2 Tuesday & Friday

Behaviour

Comment: Emma is a polite and attentive student who is usually motivated and interested in class. She is more comfortable asking questions when she has time to think, and she becomes more confident when she is given more time or when answers are not given straight away. At times, she can seem a little uneasy or defensive when she does not understand something, but she is willing to make more effort and try when encouraged.

Work

Comment: Emma takes part well in activities and works seriously. She uses the target language well in gapped sentences and can use grammatical structures from previous chapters. She participates well in collaborative work and is more willing to speak when supported, although she still sometimes tries to avoid speaking and needs encouragement to expand her answers. She also sometimes asks for vocabulary in French and needs help with pronunciation, vocabulary, and writing tasks.

Performance

She has also shown that she can use structures such as the causative form, comparatives, would rather...than..., prefer...to..., and the language needed to create problems and solutions. She still makes some pronunciation, pronoun, tense, third-person singular -s, spelling, capital letter, and punctuation mistakes, and she is not always confident when giving full answers or using new vocabulary in original sentences.

Final comment

Emma is a polite and attentive student who has become more confident when given time and support. She can use several target language structures well and reads impressively. Kids&Us.

COURSE OBJECTIVES - FAIRY TALES 2, 3rd term

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Emma
Teacher: Huryv Osmanova
Level: Fairy Tales 2 Tuesday & Friday 2025/2026
Absences: 0

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Excellent

Emma is a polite and well-mannered student who generally shows a positive attitude in class. She is calm, respectful, and attentive, and she works well with both her teacher and classmates. At times, she can be a little hesitant to speak up, but with encouragement she is willing to try and becomes more confident. She responds well when given time to think and can be very receptive to guidance. Her kind and cooperative nature contributes positively to the classroom atmosphere.

Work

Assessment (Work): Good

Emma takes part in class activities and usually completes the work she is asked to do with care. She has shown good effort throughout the term, especially when speaking and working with familiar structures. She can make sentences using the target language and is able to follow the lesson content well. However, she sometimes needs encouragement to try more independently and to avoid relying too much on French. Continuing to build confidence and expanding her vocabulary will help her participate more freely and consistently.

Performance

Assessment (Performance): Good

Emma has made good progress this term. She understands the new vocabulary well and can recognise and use many of the structures covered in class. She is also able to read the stories and chapters well, and her reading is often impressive. In oral activities, she can answer using full structures and usually corrects herself when she makes mistakes, which is very positive. She still needs to work on pronunciation, connectors, and some grammar points such as the third person singular, pronouns, and tense accuracy. With regular practice and more confidence, she will continue to improve.

Marks

Oral Test Mark: 7.10 Good.

Emma performs well orally when she feels comfortable and has enough time to think. She can repeat and use the target language accurately in many situations, and her pronunciation is generally improving. However, she still makes occasional pronunciation mistakes and sometimes needs support to expand her answers.

Writing Mark: 6.80 Good.

Emma's written work shows a good understanding of the language covered this term. She can complete grammar and vocabulary tasks quite well. However, she still makes some errors with capital letters, punctuation, and grammar when writing independently. Further practice with writing and careful revision before tests will help her gain more confidence and accuracy.

Homework mark: 6.50 Acceptable.

Emma has completed some homework and has generally shown a reasonable effort. To improve further, she should make a more consistent effort to practise at home, revise vocabulary regularly, and review the grammar points covered in class.

Final comment

Over the course of the term, Emma has shown a positive attitude and has made steady progress in class. She is polite, attentive, and increasingly willing to try, especially when she is given a bit of time and support. Her reading skills are particularly strong, and she shows good understanding of the content covered in class. To continue improving, she should focus on speaking more confidently, using English as much as possible, and revising vocabulary and grammar regularly. With continued encouragement and practice, Emma is well placed to make further progress next term.

The report must follow the three-section structure (Behaviour, Work, Performance) plus Marks and a Final comment, as shown in the examples. Each section should include an Assessment rating and a Comment paragraph. All observations must be grounded in the source notes. Score 1-5 on: - structure: three sections (Behaviour/Work/Performance) + Marks + Final comment - grounding: every statement traceable to notes (no invented observations) - tone: constructive and honest, matches examples - balance: weaknesses (pronunciation errors, reliance on French, avoiding speaking) acknowledged alongside strengths "}]

response.output:
Kids&Us.

COURSE OBJECTIVES - FAIRY TALES 2, Tuesday & Friday

---

Term report

Data

  • Name: Emma
  • Class: Fairy Tales 2 Tuesday & Friday

Behaviour

Comment: Emma is a polite and attentive student who is usually motivated and interested in class. She is more comfortable asking questions when she has time to think, and she becomes more confident when she is given more time or when answers are not given straight away. At times, she can seem a little uneasy or defensive when she does not understand something, but she is willing to make more effort and try when encouraged.

Work

Comment: Emma takes part well in activities and works seriously. She uses the target language well in gapped sentences and can use grammatical structures from previous chapters. She participates well in collaborative work and is more willing to speak when supported, although she still sometimes tries to avoid speaking and needs encouragement to expand her answers. She also sometimes asks for vocabulary in French and needs help with pronunciation, vocabulary, and writing tasks.

Performance

Comment: Emma reads very well, and her reading was particularly impressive. She can summarise chapters, answer questions, use the target language from the board, and manage listening activities well. She has also shown that she can use structures such as the causative form, comparatives, would rather...than..., prefer...to..., and the language needed to create problems and solutions. She still makes some pronunciation, pronoun, tense, third-person singular -s, spelling, capital letter, and punctuation mistakes, and she is not always confident when giving full answers or using new vocabulary in original sentences.

Final comment

Emma is a polite and attentive student who has become more confident when given time and support. She can use several target language structures well and reads impressively. With continued encouragement to try more often, speak a little more freely, and keep working on pronunciation, grammar, and vocabulary, she will continue to improve.

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Emma
Level: Fairy Tales 2 Tuesday & Friday

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Good
Comment: Emma maintains a polite and attentive demeanor in class. She is generally respectful and cooperative, participating willingly when encouraged. On occasion, she can be playful but remains focused during activities. She asks for vocabulary assistance, initially in French but is encouraged to use English, showing her eagerness to learn. Emma interacts positively with her peers and responds well to teacher guidance.

Work

Assessment (Work): Good
Comment: Emma demonstrates diligence in her work, particularly in grammar and vocabulary exercises where she performs well with minor reminders needed for structures like the "-ing" form after "used to." She participates actively in collaborative tasks and can use the target language in structured activities. However, she sometimes avoids speaking and individual assignments, preferring to seek assistance or take breaks. Her writing shows some issues with capitalization and punctuation, and she occasionally struggles with forming sentences using new vocabulary to demonstrate understanding. With encouragement, she makes efforts to expand her answers and corrects her mistakes when prompted.

Performance

Assessment (Performance): Good
Comment: Emma’s reading skills are notably strong, with impressive fluency and comprehension, though not always entirely fluent. She understands and responds to questions about texts and songs, and can use complex structures such as "would rather...than..." and "prefer...to..." She participates effectively in discussions about topics like food groups and meals, using provided structures well. However, she occasionally makes pronunciation errors and tense mistakes, such as using "do" for "he/she." Her ability to recall and apply grammar points like connectors ("although," "despite") and causative forms ("get something done") varies, showing improvement with practice. Emma benefits from peer support, such as from Romain and Helena, and demonstrates growing confidence in her oral skills over time.

Final comment

Emma is a polite and attentive student who shows enthusiasm for learning, particularly in reading and collaborative tasks. Her willingness to participate improves when given time and encouragement, and she actively seeks clarification when needed. While she excels in certain areas like reading and using target language structures, she would benefit from greater consistency in individual work and speaking practice. Focusing on pronunciation, grammar accuracy, and confidence in forming sentences with new vocabulary will enhance her overall performance. With continued encouragement and targeted practice, Emma has the potential to make further progress.

COURSE OBJECTIVES - FAIRY TALES 2, 3rd term

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Emma
Teacher: Huryv Osmanova
Level: Fairy Tales 2 Tuesday & Friday 2025/2026
Absences: 0

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Excellent

Emma is a polite and well-mannered student who generally shows a positive attitude in class. She is calm, respectful, and attentive, and she works well with both her teacher and classmates. At times, she can be a little hesitant to speak up, but with encouragement she is willing to try and becomes more confident. She responds well when given time to think and can be very receptive to guidance. Her kind and cooperative nature contributes positively to the classroom atmosphere.

Work

Assessment (Work): Good

Emma takes part in class activities and usually completes the work she is asked to do with care. She has shown good effort throughout the term, especially when speaking and working with familiar structures. She can make sentences using the target language and is able to follow the lesson content well. However, she sometimes needs encouragement to try more independently and to avoid relying too much on French. Continuing to build confidence and expanding her vocabulary will help her participate more freely and consistently.

Performance

Assessment (Performance): Good

Emma has made good progress this term. She understands the new vocabulary well and can recognise and use many of the structures covered in class. She is also able to read the stories and chapters well, and her reading is often impressive. In oral activities, she can answer using full structures and usually corrects herself when she makes mistakes, which is very positive. She still needs to work on pronunciation, connectors, and some grammar points such as the third person singular, pronouns, and tense accuracy. With regular practice and more confidence, she will continue to improve.

Marks

Oral Test Mark: 7.10 Good.

Emma performs well orally when she feels comfortable and has enough time to think. She can repeat and use the target language accurately in many situations, and her pronunciation is generally improving. However, she still makes occasional pronunciation mistakes and sometimes needs support to expand her answers.

Writing Mark: 6.80 Good.

Emma's written work shows a good understanding of the language covered this term. She can complete grammar and vocabulary tasks quite well. However, she still makes some errors with capital letters, punctuation, and grammar when writing independently. Further practice with writing and careful revision before tests will help her gain more confidence and accuracy.

Homework mark: 6.50 Acceptable.

Emma has completed some homework and has generally shown a reasonable effort. To improve further, she should make a more consistent effort to practise at home, revise vocabulary regularly, and review the grammar points covered in class.

Final comment

Over the course of the term, Emma has shown a positive attitude and has made steady progress in class. She is polite, attentive, and increasingly willing to try, especially when she is given a bit of time and support. Her reading skills are particularly strong, and she shows good understanding of the content covered in class. To continue improving, she should focus on speaking more confidently, using English as much as possible, and revising vocabulary and grammar regularly. With continued encouragement and practice, Emma is well placed to make further progress next term.

The report must follow the three-section structure (Behaviour, Work, Performance) plus Marks and a Final comment, as shown in the examples. Each section should include an Assessment rating and a Comment paragraph. All observations must be grounded in the source notes. Score 1-5 on: - structure: three sections (Behaviour/Work/Performance) + Marks + Final comment - grounding: every statement traceable to notes (no invented observations) - tone: constructive and honest, matches examples - balance: weaknesses (pronunciation errors, reliance on French, avoiding speaking) acknowledged alongside strengths "}]

COURSE OBJECTIVES - FAIRY TALES 2, 3rd term

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Emma
Teacher: Huryv Osmanova
Level: Fairy Tales 2 Tuesday & Friday 2025/2026
Absences: 0

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Excellent

Emma is a polite and well-mannered student who generally shows a positive attitude in class. She is calm, respectful, and attentive, and she works well with both her teacher and classmates. At times, she can be a little hesitant to speak up, but with encouragement she is willing to try and becomes more confident. She responds well when given time to think and can be very receptive to guidance. Her kind and cooperative nature contributes positively to the classroom atmosphere.

Work

Assessment (Work): Good

Emma takes part in class activities and usually completes the work she is asked to do with care. She has shown good effort throughout the term, especially when speaking and working with familiar structures. She can make sentences using the target language and is able to follow the lesson content well. However, she sometimes needs encouragement to try more independently and to avoid relying too much on French. Continuing to build confidence and expanding her vocabulary will help her participate more freely and consistently.

Performance

Assessment (Performance): Good

Emma has made good progress this term. She understands the new vocabulary well and can recognise and use many of the structures covered in class. She is also able to read the stories and chapters well, and her reading is often impressive. In oral activities, she can answer using full structures and usually corrects herself when she makes mistakes, which is very positive. She still needs to work on pronunciation, connectors, and some grammar points such as the third person singular, pronouns, and tense accuracy. With regular practice and more confidence, she will continue to improve.

Marks

Oral Test Mark: 7.10 Good.

Emma performs well orally when she feels comfortable and has enough time to think. She can repeat and use the target language accurately in many situations, and her pronunciation is generally improving. However, she still makes occasional pronunciation mistakes and sometimes needs support to expand her answers.

Writing Mark: 6.80 Good.

Emma's written work shows a good understanding of the language covered this term. She can complete grammar and vocabulary tasks quite well. However, she still makes some errors with capital letters, punctuation, and grammar when writing independently. Further practice with writing and careful revision before tests will help her gain more confidence and accuracy.

Homework mark: 6.50 Acceptable.

Emma has completed some homework and has generally shown a reasonable effort. To improve further, she should make a more consistent effort to practise at home, revise vocabulary regularly, and review the grammar points covered in class.

Final comment

Over the course of the term, Emma has shown a positive attitude and has made steady progress in class. She is polite, attentive, and increasingly willing to try, especially when she is given a bit of time and support. Her reading skills are particularly strong, and she shows good understanding of the content covered in class. To continue improving, she should focus on speaking more confidently, using English as much as possible, and revising vocabulary and grammar regularly. With continued encouragement and practice, Emma is well placed to make further progress next term.

The report must follow the three-section structure (Behaviour, Work, Performance) plus Marks and a Final comment, as shown in the examples. Each section should include an Assessment rating and a Comment paragraph. All observations must be grounded in the source notes. Score 1-5 on: - structure: three sections (Behaviour/Work/Performance) + Marks + Final comment - grounding: every statement traceable to notes (no invented observations) - tone: constructive and honest, matches examples - balance: weaknesses (pronunciation errors, reliance on French, avoiding speaking) acknowledged alongside strengths

Every observation, data field, and mark must come from the notes — not from any examples. ## Style & Layout Guide The following are example report cards. Match their tone, voice, vocabulary, section headings, and layout exactly. These examples may differ slightly from the provided additional instructions - in that case, the instructions from the user take precedence. IMPORTANT: Do not copy specific Data field values, Marks, or observations from the examples — only include a Data field or Marks section if that value is present in the student notes. ### Example 1: Informes 3r trimestre Alice Ling Excellent.pdf Kids&Us. COURSE OBJECTIVES - FAIRY TALES 1, 3rd term --- Term report (3rd term) Data Name: Alice Ling Teacher: Huryv Osmanova Level: Tweens 3 2024/2025 Absences: 0 Behaviour Assessment (Behaviour): Excellent Comment: Alice continues to be a kind, responsible, and respectful student. She is always polite and considerate towards both her classmates and teachers. She actively listens, engages in class discussions, and consistently asks for clarification when she doesn't understand something. Her positive and cooperative nature contributes to a welcoming and supportive classroom environment. Work Assessment (Work): Excellent Comment: Alice remains an engaged and hardworking student. She participates actively in class and completes her tasks with enthusiasm. She consistently takes initiative in her learning and demonstrates great responsibility. However, she should make an effort to revise more regularly and complete all assigned homework to reinforce her understanding of the material. Performance Assessment (Performance): Excellent Comment: Alice continues to excel in her academic performance. She has achieved excellent results in her assessments and demonstrates a strong grasp of vocabulary, grammar, and comprehension. Her test results are consistently impressive, and she shows great confidence in both oral and written tasks. Marks Oral Test Mark: 10.00 Excellent. Writing Mark: 9.80 Excellent. Homework mark: 0.00 Needs to improve. --- Final comment Alice has proven to be a bright, motivated, and dedicated student. She consistently demonstrates a strong command of English and maintains excellent behaviour and participation in class. With improved consistency in completing homework and regular revision, she is well-positioned to continue excelling. ### Example 2: Informes 3r trimestre Romain Fafchamps Good.pdf Kids&Us. COURSE OBJECTIVES - FAIRY TALES 1, 3rd term --- Term report (3rd term) Data Name: Romain Fachamps Teacher: Huryu Osmanova Level: Tweens 3 2024/2025 Absences: 0 Behaviour Assessment (Behaviour): Excellent Comment: Romain continues to be a kind, respectful, and responsible student. Performance Assessment (Performance): Excellent Comment: Ylena performed very well in her assessments. She is a confident speaker and expresses her thoughts clearly. She has a strong grasp of vocabulary and sentence structures but should continue to revise certain grammar points for greater accuracy. Marks Oral Test Mark: 9.40 Excellent. Writing Mark: 8.70 Very good. Homework mark: 0.00 Needs to improve. --- Final comment Ylena has shown consistent dedication throughout the year. She maintains a positive attitude, shows respect to her peers and teacher, and takes initiative in her learning. Her strong speaking and writing skills reflect her overall engagement. With improved homework habits and continued focus on grammar and vocabulary, she has great potential to reach excellence across all areas. ## Student Notes (source of truth — all report content must derive from these) Student: Emma, Class: Fairy Tales 2 Tuesday & Friday - 2026-05-14: Emma came very late, 25 minutes late, and she left early. She was in a rather good mood, but she didn't understand some of the vocabulary, so she asked me to tell some of the words in French. She looked a bit desperate because she didn't understand what was going on, so she was a bit upset about that. When she saw that it was a writing task, she looked very happy not to have to do it because she was leaving at that time. But she did the grammar vocabulary very well, and she only needed a little reminder that after used to, we had to use a verb in -ing. - 2026-05-14: Emma was the only student who came to class because Alice, Romain and Hélène were absent. The class went really well: she was much more confident, could take her time to answer, and looked very comfortable asking me questions. She is more willing to make efforts and try to get the right answers when I give her more time or do not give her the answers straight away. She was very attentive and polite, like always, and she read the text quite well; her reading was really, really impressive. She still sometimes makes errors when she pronounces some words, but I told her I found her very good today and that I was very happy and proud of her. - 2026-05-14: Emma could use some of the grammatical structures and remembered a little bit of the previous chapters. She also sometimes makes mistakes when she speaks, especially with pronouns, and sometimes forgets tenses or the third-person singular -s. - 2026-05-14: Helena helped her a lot with the vocabulary. When we worked collaboratively, Emma participated, but when it was individual, she asked to go to the loo and stayed there a bit. Emma and Romain needed a bit of help for inspiration, and Emma sometimes needed a bit of help with pronunciation. She knew how to use the connector but had to be reminded of its purpose. - 2026-05-14: Emma made some efforts to talk a bit more and expand her answers, being more confident. response.output:
```html

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Emma
Level: Fairy Tales 2 Tuesday & Friday

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Good
Comment: Emma maintains a polite and attentive demeanor in class. She is generally respectful and cooperative, participating willingly when encouraged. On occasion, she can be playful but remains focused during activities. She asks for vocabulary assistance, initially in French but is encouraged to use English, showing her eagerness to learn. Emma interacts positively with her peers and responds well to teacher guidance.

Work

Assessment (Work): Good
Comment: Emma demonstrates diligence in her work, particularly in grammar and vocabulary exercises where she performs well with minor reminders needed for structures like the "-ing" form after "used to." She participates actively in collaborative tasks and can use the target language in structured activities. However, she sometimes avoids speaking and individual assignments, preferring to seek assistance or take breaks. Her writing shows some issues with capitalization and punctuation, and she occasionally struggles with forming sentences using new vocabulary to demonstrate understanding. With encouragement, she makes efforts to expand her answers and corrects her mistakes when prompted.

Performance

Assessment (Performance): Good
Comment: Emma’s reading skills are notably strong, with impressive fluency and comprehension, though not always entirely fluent. She understands and responds to questions about texts and songs, and can use complex structures such as "would rather...than..." and "prefer...to..." She participates effectively in discussions about topics like food groups and meals, using provided structures well. However, she occasionally makes pronunciation errors and tense mistakes, such as using "do" for "he/she." Her ability to recall and apply grammar points like connectors ("although," "despite") and causative forms ("get something done") varies, showing improvement with practice. Emma benefits from peer support, such as from Romain and Helena, and demonstrates growing confidence in her oral skills over time.

Final comment

Emma is a polite and attentive student who shows enthusiasm for learning, particularly in reading and collaborative tasks. Her willingness to participate improves when given time and encouragement, and she actively seeks clarification when needed. While she excels in certain areas like reading and using target language structures, she would benefit from greater consistency in individual work and speaking practice. Focusing on pronunciation, grammar accuracy, and confidence in forming sentences with new vocabulary will enhance her overall performance. With continued encouragement and targeted practice, Emma has the potential to make further progress.

```

---

Term report (3rd term)

Data

  • Name: Emma
  • Class: Fairy Tales 2 Tuesday & Friday

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Very good

Comment: Emma is a polite and attentive student who maintains a positive attitude in class. She is respectful toward her teacher and classmates, though she occasionally appears uneasy. Her mood is generally good, and she engages well when given time and encouragement. She works calmly and collaborates effectively with peers like Romain and Helena, showing improved confidence when working in smaller groups.

Work

Assessment (Work): Good

Comment: Emma demonstrates effort and participation in class activities, particularly when given support or additional time. She engages well in collaborative tasks and shows willingness to improve, though she sometimes hesitates to attempt tasks independently. Her focus is strong when motivated, and she benefits from encouragement to expand her answers. She occasionally avoids speaking or writing tasks but completes them when guided. Her reading skills are impressive, though she relies on vocabulary support from peers or the teacher.

Performance

Assessment (Performance): Good

Her pronunciation has improved but still requires attention, particularly with third-person singular "-s" and tense consistency. She performs well in listening and reading tasks, summarizing chapters and answering questions accurately. Her writing reflects understanding but includes minor errors in punctuation and capitalization. While she sometimes struggles with vocabulary recall, she uses target language effectively when supported.

Marks

Oral Test Mark: Not assessed this term (limited individual speaking opportunities due to absences of peers).

Writing Mark: Not formally assessed (tasks partially completed or avoided).

---

Emma has made noticeable progress this term, particularly in her reading fluency and use of grammatical structures. She is polite, attentive, and increasingly confident when given time and encouragement. While she sometimes hesitates to speak or write independently, she shows strong potential when collaborating with peers or receiving targeted support. To further her development, Emma should focus on expanding her vocabulary, practicing pronunciation, and building confidence in independent tasks. Her positive attitude and willingness to improve are commendable, and with continued effort, she will strengthen her overall language skills.

Kids&Us.

COURSE OBJECTIVES - FAIRY TALES 2, 3rd term

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Emma
Teacher: Huryv Osmanova
Level: Fairy Tales 2 Tuesday & Friday 2025/2026
Absences: 0

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Excellent

Emma is a polite and well-mannered student who generally shows a positive attitude in class. She is calm, respectful, and attentive, and she works well with both her teacher and classmates. At times, she can be a little hesitant to speak up, but with encouragement she is willing to try and becomes more confident. She responds well when given time to think and can be very receptive to guidance. Her kind and cooperative nature contributes positively to the classroom atmosphere.

Work

Assessment (Work): Good

Emma takes part in class activities and usually completes the work she is asked to do with care. She has shown good effort throughout the term, especially when speaking and working with familiar structures. She can make sentences using the target language and is able to follow the lesson content well. However, she sometimes needs encouragement to try more independently and to avoid relying too much on French. Continuing to build confidence and expanding her vocabulary will help her participate more freely and consistently.

Performance

Assessment (Performance): Good

Emma has made good progress this term. She understands the new vocabulary well and can recognise and use many of the structures covered in class. She is also able to read the stories and chapters well, and her reading is often impressive. In oral activities, she can answer using full structures and usually corrects herself when she makes mistakes, which is very positive. She still needs to work on pronunciation, connectors, and some grammar points such as the third person singular, pronouns, and tense accuracy. With regular practice and more confidence, she will continue to improve.

Marks

Oral Test Mark: 7.10 Good.

Emma performs well orally when she feels comfortable and has enough time to think. She can repeat and use the target language accurately in many situations, and her pronunciation is generally improving. However, she still makes occasional pronunciation mistakes and sometimes needs support to expand her answers.

Writing Mark: 6.80 Good.

Emma's written work shows a good understanding of the language covered this term. She can complete grammar and vocabulary tasks quite well. However, she still makes some errors with capital letters, punctuation, and grammar when writing independently. Further practice with writing and careful revision before tests will help her gain more confidence and accuracy.

Homework mark: 6.50 Acceptable.

Emma has completed some homework and has generally shown a reasonable effort. To improve further, she should make a more consistent effort to practise at home, revise vocabulary regularly, and review the grammar points covered in class.

Final comment

Over the course of the term, Emma has shown a positive attitude and has made steady progress in class. She is polite, attentive, and increasingly willing to try, especially when she is given a bit of time and support. Her reading skills are particularly strong, and she shows good understanding of the content covered in class. To continue improving, she should focus on speaking more confidently, using English as much as possible, and revising vocabulary and grammar regularly. With continued encouragement and practice, Emma is well placed to make further progress next term.

The report must follow the three-section structure (Behaviour, Work, Performance) plus Marks and a Final comment, as shown in the examples. Each section should include an Assessment rating and a Comment paragraph. All observations must be grounded in the source notes. Score 1-5 on: - structure: three sections (Behaviour/Work/Performance) + Marks + Final comment - grounding: every statement traceable to notes (no invented observations) - tone: constructive and honest, matches examples - balance: weaknesses (pronunciation errors, reliance on French, avoiding speaking) acknowledged alongside strengths "}]

response.output:
```html

Kids&Us.

COURSE OBJECTIVES - FAIRY TALES 2, 3rd term

---

Term report (3rd term)

Data

  • Name: Emma
  • Class: Fairy Tales 2 Tuesday & Friday

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Very good

Comment: Emma is a polite and attentive student who maintains a positive attitude in class. She is respectful toward her teacher and classmates, though she occasionally appears uneasy. Her mood is generally good, and she engages well when given time and encouragement. She works calmly and collaborates effectively with peers like Romain and Helena, showing improved confidence when working in smaller groups.

Work

Assessment (Work): Good

Comment: Emma demonstrates effort and participation in class activities, particularly when given support or additional time. She engages well in collaborative tasks and shows willingness to improve, though she sometimes hesitates to attempt tasks independently. Her focus is strong when motivated, and she benefits from encouragement to expand her answers. She occasionally avoids speaking or writing tasks but completes them when guided. Her reading skills are impressive, though she relies on vocabulary support from peers or the teacher.

Performance

Assessment (Performance): Good

Comment: Emma shows a solid understanding of grammatical structures, such as the causative form ("get something done"), comparatives, and connectors like "although" and "despite," though she occasionally needs reminders. Her pronunciation has improved but still requires attention, particularly with third-person singular "-s" and tense consistency. She performs well in listening and reading tasks, summarizing chapters and answering questions accurately. Her writing reflects understanding but includes minor errors in punctuation and capitalization. While she sometimes struggles with vocabulary recall, she uses target language effectively when supported.

Marks

Oral Test Mark: Not assessed this term (limited individual speaking opportunities due to absences of peers).

Writing Mark: Not formally assessed (tasks partially completed or avoided).

---

Final comment

Emma has made noticeable progress this term, particularly in her reading fluency and use of grammatical structures. She is polite, attentive, and increasingly confident when given time and encouragement. While she sometimes hesitates to speak or write independently, she shows strong potential when collaborating with peers or receiving targeted support. To further her development, Emma should focus on expanding her vocabulary, practicing pronunciation, and building confidence in independent tasks. Her positive attitude and willingness to improve are commendable, and with continued effort, she will strengthen her overall language skills.

```

Data

Name: Emma
Teacher: Huryv Osmanova
Level: Fairy Tales 2 Tuesday & Friday 2025/2026

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Excellent

Emma is a polite and well-mannered student who generally shows a positive attitude in class. She is calm, respectful, and attentive, and she works well with both her teacher and classmates. At times, she can be a little hesitant to speak up, but with encouragement she is willing to try and becomes more confident. She responds well when given time to think and can be very receptive to guidance. Her kind and cooperative nature contributes positively to the classroom atmosphere.

Work

Assessment (Work): Good

Emma takes part in class activities and usually completes the work she is asked to do with care. She has shown good effort throughout the term, especially when speaking and working with familiar structures. She can make sentences using the target language and is able to follow the lesson content well. However, she sometimes needs encouragement to try more independently and to avoid relying too much on French. Continuing to build confidence and expanding her vocabulary will help her participate more freely and consistently.

Performance

Assessment (Performance): Good

Emma has made good progress this term. She understands the new vocabulary well and can recognise and use many of the structures covered in class. She is also able to read the stories and chapters well, and her reading is often impressive. In oral activities, she can answer using full structures and usually corrects herself when she makes mistakes, which is very positive. She still needs to work on pronunciation, connectors, and some grammar points such as the third person singular, pronouns, and tense accuracy. With regular practice and more confidence, she will continue to improve.

Marks

Oral Test Mark: 7.10 Good.

Emma performs well orally when she feels comfortable and has enough time to think. She can repeat and use the target language accurately in many situations, and her pronunciation is generally improving. However, she still makes occasional pronunciation mistakes and sometimes needs support to expand her answers.

Writing Mark: 6.80 Good.

Emma's written work shows a good understanding of the language covered this term. She can complete grammar and vocabulary tasks quite well. However, she still makes some errors with capital letters, punctuation, and grammar when writing independently. Further practice with writing and careful revision before tests will help her gain more confidence and accuracy.

Homework mark: 6.50 Acceptable.

Emma has completed some homework and has generally shown a reasonable effort. To improve further, she should make a more consistent effort to practise at home, revise vocabulary regularly, and review the grammar points covered in class.

Final comment

Over the course of the term, Emma has shown a positive attitude and has made steady progress in class. She is polite, attentive, and increasingly willing to try, especially when she is given a bit of time and support. Her reading skills are particularly strong, and she shows good understanding of the content covered in class. To continue improving, she should focus on speaking more confidently, using English as much as possible, and revising vocabulary and grammar regularly. With continued encouragement and practice, Emma is well placed to make further progress next term.

COURSE OBJECTIVES - FAIRY TALES 2, 3rd term

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Emma
Teacher: Huryv Osmanova
Level: Fairy Tales 2 Tuesday & Friday 2025/2026
Absences: 0

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Excellent

Emma is a polite and well-mannered student who generally shows a positive attitude in class. She is calm, respectful, and attentive, and she works well with both her teacher and classmates. At times, she can be a little hesitant to speak up, but with encouragement she is willing to try and becomes more confident. She responds well when given time to think and can be very receptive to guidance. Her kind and cooperative nature contributes positively to the classroom atmosphere.

Work

Assessment (Work): Good

Emma takes part in class activities and usually completes the work she is asked to do with care. She has shown good effort throughout the term, especially when speaking and working with familiar structures. She can make sentences using the target language and is able to follow the lesson content well. However, she sometimes needs encouragement to try more independently and to avoid relying too much on French. Continuing to build confidence and expanding her vocabulary will help her participate more freely and consistently.

Performance

Assessment (Performance): Good

Emma has made good progress this term. She understands the new vocabulary well and can recognise and use many of the structures covered in class. She is also able to read the stories and chapters well, and her reading is often impressive. In oral activities, she can answer using full structures and usually corrects herself when she makes mistakes, which is very positive. She still needs to work on pronunciation, connectors, and some grammar points such as the third person singular, pronouns, and tense accuracy. With regular practice and more confidence, she will continue to improve.

Marks

Oral Test Mark: 7.10 Good.

Emma performs well orally when she feels comfortable and has enough time to think. She can repeat and use the target language accurately in many situations, and her pronunciation is generally improving. However, she still makes occasional pronunciation mistakes and sometimes needs support to expand her answers.

Writing Mark: 6.80 Good.

Emma's written work shows a good understanding of the language covered this term. She can complete grammar and vocabulary tasks quite well. However, she still makes some errors with capital letters, punctuation, and grammar when writing independently. Further practice with writing and careful revision before tests will help her gain more confidence and accuracy.

Homework mark: 6.50 Acceptable.

Emma has completed some homework and has generally shown a reasonable effort. To improve further, she should make a more consistent effort to practise at home, revise vocabulary regularly, and review the grammar points covered in class.

Final comment

Over the course of the term, Emma has shown a positive attitude and has made steady progress in class. She is polite, attentive, and increasingly willing to try, especially when she is given a bit of time and support. Her reading skills are particularly strong, and she shows good understanding of the content covered in class. To continue improving, she should focus on speaking more confidently, using English as much as possible, and revising vocabulary and grammar regularly. With continued encouragement and practice, Emma is well placed to make further progress next term.

The report must follow the three-section structure (Behaviour, Work, Performance) plus Marks and a Final comment, as shown in the examples. Each section should include an Assessment rating and a Comment paragraph. All observations must be grounded in the source notes. Score 1-5 on: - structure: three sections (Behaviour/Work/Performance) + Marks + Final comment - grounding: every statement traceable to notes (no invented observations) - tone: constructive and honest, matches examples - balance: weaknesses (pronunciation errors, reliance on French, avoiding speaking) acknowledged alongside strengths

Kids&Us.

COURSE OBJECTIVES - FAIRY TALES 2, 3rd term

---

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Emma
Teacher: [Not specified in notes]
Level: Fairy Tales 2
Class: Tuesday & Friday

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Good

Comment: Emma is generally polite, attentive, and motivated in class. She maintains a positive attitude and is cooperative with both the teacher and her classmates. She sometimes appears uneasy but becomes more comfortable as the class progresses. She is willing to ask for clarification when needed, though she occasionally resorts to French for vocabulary.

Work

Assessment (Work): Good

Comment: Emma participates in class activities and demonstrates effort in her tasks. She is more confident when given time to answer and shows willingness to try when encouraged. She sometimes avoids speaking or writing tasks but engages when prompted. She works well collaboratively but occasionally seeks distractions during individual work.

Performance

Assessment (Performance): Good

Comment: Emma shows a solid understanding of grammar structures, such as the causative form, comparatives, and target language from previous chapters. She reads texts well, with impressive clarity, though she occasionally struggles with pronunciation and fluency. She can summarize chapters, form questions, and use target language structures effectively. However, she sometimes makes mistakes with tenses, pronouns, and punctuation. Her vocabulary recall is inconsistent, and she often requests translations in French.

Marks

Oral Test Mark: [Not specified in notes]
Writing Mark: [Not specified in notes]
Homework mark: [Not specified in notes]

---

Final comment

Emma has shown consistent effort and engagement in class. She demonstrates a good grasp of many grammatical structures and reads with clarity. With continued encouragement to speak and write in English, as well as more consistent use of target vocabulary, she can further improve her confidence and accuracy. Reducing reliance on French for vocabulary and focusing on pronunciation and tense accuracy will help her progress.

Does not speak like a pirate {"reason": "'avast ye' is a common pirate term", "pass": false, "score": 0.0}"},{"role":"user","content":"

Kids&Us.

Objectives and content of the Emma course, 3rd term

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Chloé
Teacher: [Not provided in notes]
Level: Emma Saturday
Absences: [Not provided in notes]

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Very good

Chloé usually comes to class in a good mood. She participates enthusiastically in all the activities. Chloé enjoys story time and can repeat the story with the classmates and the teacher. She also shows a real interest in the worksheet activities and the "what's missing" game. Chloé can tell her age and gender, often using the complete structure. She can pair up Emma’s dirty/clean body parts and place tattoos on the correct body parts with a little help. Chloé can also pair up the correct actions in the correct rooms and repeat new vocabulary words and structures. She is usually cooperative with her teacher and her classmates.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Satisfactory

Chloé’s English knowledge is satisfactory. She knows some vocabulary and structures but sometimes needs the teacher’s help to repeat full structures and use words in sentences. This term, she specifically learnt the different actions and the different rooms/places in which those actions take place. She has also reinforced her knowledge of the body parts, the clothes items, and the concepts of clean/dirty and dry/wet. Chloé can count well and understand the corresponding colour and clothing item on her bingo card. She can put clothes items on and take them off, and with help, she can put the correct coloured items. Chloé can pair up the correct actions in the correct rooms and repeat new vocabulary words and structures.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Good

Chloé is a friendly and energetic student. She treats her classmates and her teacher with respect and care. She listens attentively in class and usually stays focused during the lesson, though she can be distracted at times. Chloé has learnt to stay more focused and listen more attentively to the teacher, following instructions rather well. She takes good care of the material and usually shares it with her classmates. On a few occasions, she asked repetitive questions, which could be distracting, but she stopped when reminded.

Final comment

Throughout the term, Chloé has shown enthusiasm and engagement in the classroom activities. She has made progress in her English learning, developing her vocabulary and comprehension skills. Chloé’s willingness to participate and her cooperative attitude have contributed to a positive learning environment. It is recommended to continue practising the vocabulary and structures learnt this term to reinforce her progress.

Objectives and content of the Emma course, 3rd term

---

Term report (3rd term)

Data

  • Name: Chloé
  • Teacher: [Teacher]
  • Level: Emma Saturday 2025/2026

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Very good

Comment:
Chloé usually comes to class in a good mood and is generally willing to take part in the activities proposed. She is open to new tasks and, once reminded of the classroom routine, she joins in with enthusiasm. At the beginning of some sessions, she could be a little loud or repeatedly ask for confirmation, but she usually settles well when given clear guidance. Chloé is cooperative with the teacher and with her classmates, and she especially enjoys hands-on activities such as worksheet work, bingo, cards and dressing Emma.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Satisfactory

Comment:
Chloé's English knowledge is satisfactory. She can repeat the new structures well and often understands the language being worked on in class. This term, she worked on colours, clothes, body parts, actions, rooms and places, as well as the concepts of clean/dirty and dry/wet. She can usually tell her age and gender and, on several occasions, used the complete structure correctly. She was also able to pair actions with the correct rooms and take part in the story-time activities. However, she still needs more practice with some vocabulary items, especially clothes and colours, and she sometimes needs the teacher's help to remember or apply the full structure. With more regular revision, she will continue to gain confidence and accuracy.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Good

Comment:
Chloé is a lively and generally well-integrated student. She can be a little distracted or impatient at times, especially when she is tired or when the group is especially active, but she responds well to reminders and class management strategies. Once the rules are clear, she behaves appropriately and stays focused on the task. She usually treats the material carefully and works well with the rest of the group. Her attitude in class is positive and she is able to adapt well to the pace of the lesson.

Final comment

Throughout the term, Chloé has shown a positive attitude towards the classroom routines and has taken part well in the activities. She is able to understand and repeat a good part of the language worked on in class, and she is becoming more confident when using complete structures. She still needs to keep reinforcing some vocabulary, especially clothes and colours, but she is making good progress. Well done, Chloé! It is recommended to keep listening to the audio tracks and revising the vocabulary at home in order to consolidate the language learnt during the term.

The report must follow the three-section structure (Participation/Motivation, Learning process, Behaviour) plus a Final comment, as shown in the reference. Each section should include an Assessment rating and a Comment paragraph. The report should synthesise observations from multiple notes into a cohesive narrative. IMPORTANT: Do not invent Data fields (Absences, Frequency of use, Teacher name, Oral Test Mark, or other numerical/structured metadata) — only include facts traceable to the source notes. Score 1-5 on: - structure: three sections + Final comment, each with Assessment and Comment - grounding: every statement traceable to notes (no invented observations, no fabricated marks, absences counts, or audio-frequency values) - synthesis: observations from multiple notes woven together, not a note-by-note list - tone: warm, encouraging, matches Emma-level examples "}]

Chloé is cooperative with the teacher and with her classmates, and she especially enjoys hands-on activities such as worksheet work, bingo, cards and dressing Emma.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Satisfactory

Comment:
Chloé's English knowledge is satisfactory. She can repeat the new structures well and often understands the language being worked on in class. This term, she worked on colours, clothes, body parts, actions, rooms and places, as well as the concepts of clean/dirty and dry/wet. She can usually tell her age and gender and, on several occasions, used the complete structure correctly. She was also able to pair actions with the correct rooms and take part in the story-time activities. However, she still needs more practice with some vocabulary items, especially clothes and colours, and she sometimes needs the teacher's help to remember or apply the full structure. With more regular revision, she will continue to gain confidence and accuracy.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Good

Comment:
Chloé is a lively and generally well-integrated student. She can be a little distracted or impatient at times, especially when she is tired or when the group is especially active, but she responds well to reminders and class management strategies. Once the rules are clear, she behaves appropriately and stays focused on the task. She usually treats the material carefully and works well with the rest of the group. Her attitude in class is positive and she is able to adapt well to the pace of the lesson.

Final comment

Throughout the term, Chloé has shown a positive attitude towards the classroom routines and has taken part well in the activities. She is able to understand and repeat a good part of the language worked on in class, and she is becoming more confident when using complete structures. She still needs to keep reinforcing some vocabulary, especially clothes and colours, but she is making good progress. Well done, Chloé! It is recommended to keep listening to the audio tracks and revising the vocabulary at home in order to consolidate the language learnt during the term.

The report must follow the three-section structure (Participation/Motivation, Learning process, Behaviour) plus a Final comment, as shown in the reference. Each section should include an Assessment rating and a Comment paragraph. The report should synthesise observations from multiple notes into a cohesive narrative. IMPORTANT: Do not invent Data fields (Absences, Frequency of use, Teacher name, Oral Test Mark, or other numerical/structured metadata) — only include facts traceable to the source notes. Score 1-5 on: - structure: three sections + Final comment, each with Assessment and Comment - grounding: every statement traceable to notes (no invented observations, no fabricated marks, absences counts, or audio-frequency values) - synthesis: observations from multiple notes woven together, not a note-by-note list - tone: warm, encouraging, matches Emma-level examples
